built with pdoc

vanchor.app

Application wiring and entrypoint.

Runtime builds the whole event-driven system from interchangeable parts and starts every async loop. It is driven by an AppConfig so the same code runs the simulator, real serial hardware, or a network-fed setup -- selected by configuration, not by code changes.

Run it with::

python -m vanchor.app                       # serve the UI on :8000 (sim)
python -m vanchor.app --config my.yaml       # load a config file
python -m vanchor.app --hardware             # use real serial devices
python -m vanchor.app --nmea-tcp             # also accept phone NMEA over TCP

   1"""Application wiring and entrypoint.
   2
   3``Runtime`` builds the whole event-driven system from interchangeable parts and
   4starts every async loop. It is driven by an :class:`AppConfig` so the same code
   5runs the simulator, real serial hardware, or a network-fed setup -- selected by
   6configuration, not by code changes.
   7
   8Run it with::
   9
  10    python -m vanchor.app                       # serve the UI on :8000 (sim)
  11    python -m vanchor.app --config my.yaml       # load a config file
  12    python -m vanchor.app --hardware             # use real serial devices
  13    python -m vanchor.app --nmea-tcp             # also accept phone NMEA over TCP
  14"""
  15
  16from __future__ import annotations
  17
  18import argparse
  19import asyncio
  20import contextlib
  21import logging
  22import math
  23import os
  24import time
  25
  26from .controller.calibration import CalibrationRunner
  27from .controller.controller import Controller, Helm
  28from .controller.modes import AnchorConfig, DriftConfig, FollowApbConfig, WaypointConfig
  29from .controller.safety import SafetyConfig
  30from .core import events, observability
  31from dataclasses import asdict
  32
  33from .core.config import (
  34    AppConfig,
  35    HardwareConfig,
  36    NmeaTcpConfig,
  37    _merge_into,
  38    apply_device_overrides,
  39    load,
  40    save_device_overrides,
  41)
  42from .core.events import EventBus
  43from .core.models import BoatState, ControlModeName, Environment, GeoPoint, Waypoint
  44from .core.pid import PID
  45from .core.state import NavigationState
  46from .nav.depth import DepthMap
  47from .nav.guard import SensorGuardConfig
  48from .nav.navigator import Navigator
  49from .nav.trip import TripLog
  50from .hardware import registry
  51from .hardware.drivers import load_drivers
  52from .sim.bathymetry import Bathymetry
  53from .sim.devices import SimCompass, SimDepthSounder, SimGps
  54from .sim.simulator import Simulator
  55
  56logger = logging.getLogger("vanchor.app")
  57
  58# Populate the pluggable device-driver registry (self-registering modules under
  59# hardware/drivers/). A new driver adds itself here just by existing.
  60load_drivers()
  61
  62# Modes that count as "underway / making way" for the lost-connection failsafe
  63# (#64): every guided behaviour except idle manual and station-keeping anchor.
  64_UNDERWAY_MODES = frozenset(
  65    {
  66        ControlModeName.HEADING_HOLD,
  67        ControlModeName.WAYPOINT,
  68        ControlModeName.FOLLOW_APB,
  69        ControlModeName.DRIFT,
  70        ControlModeName.CONTOUR_FOLLOW,
  71        ControlModeName.ORBIT,
  72        ControlModeName.TROLLING,
  73    }
  74)
  75
  76
  77def _overlay_menu_values(schema: dict, saved: dict) -> dict:
  78    """Return a copy of a device-menu ``schema`` with each setting's ``value``
  79    replaced by the saved value for that key (when present) -- so the UI shows
  80    persisted choices, not just factory defaults."""
  81    settings = []
  82    for s in schema.get("settings", []):
  83        s = dict(s)
  84        if s.get("key") in saved:
  85            s["value"] = saved[s["key"]]
  86        settings.append(s)
  87    return {**schema, "settings": settings, "actions": list(schema.get("actions", []))}
  88
  89
  90def _build_boat_params(cfg: AppConfig):
  91    """Build the physics-model parameters for the configured boat geometry."""
  92    bc = cfg.boat
  93    if cfg.sim.model == "fossen":
  94        from .sim.fossen import FossenParams
  95
  96        return FossenParams(
  97            length=bc.length_m,
  98            beam=bc.beam_m,
  99            mass=bc.mass_kg,
 100            max_thrust_n=bc.max_thrust_n,
 101            reverse_efficiency=bc.reverse_efficiency,
 102            max_speed_mps=bc.max_speed_mps,
 103            thruster_x_m=bc.thruster_x_m(),
 104            thruster_y_m=bc.thruster_y_m,
 105            max_steer_angle_deg=bc.max_steer_angle_deg,
 106            hull_tracking=bc.hull_tracking,
 107        )
 108    from .sim.boat import BoatParams
 109
 110    return BoatParams(
 111        max_speed_mps=bc.max_speed_mps,
 112        max_turn_rate_deg=bc.max_turn_rate_deg,
 113        reverse_efficiency=bc.reverse_efficiency,
 114    )
 115
 116
 117def _thrust_yaw_ff_norm(cfg: AppConfig) -> float:
 118    """Thrust-yaw feed-forward as a steering-command fraction.
 119
 120    The boat config gives the cancelling deflection in radians; the helm command
 121    is a fraction of the full mechanical swing (``max_steer_angle_deg``, the same
 122    range the sim maps the command onto), so normalise by that. ``steer_sign`` is
 123    applied by the helm, not here.
 124    """
 125    bc = cfg.boat
 126    if bc.max_steer_angle_deg <= 0:
 127        return 0.0
 128    return bc.thrust_yaw_ff_angle() / math.radians(bc.max_steer_angle_deg)
 129
 130
 131def _build_battery_config(cfg: AppConfig):
 132    """Map the app `battery:` config onto the sim battery model (#60)."""
 133    from .sim.battery import BatteryConfig as SimBatteryConfig
 134
 135    b = cfg.battery
 136    return SimBatteryConfig(
 137        capacity_ah=b.capacity_ah,
 138        nominal_v=b.nominal_v,
 139        reserve_pct=b.reserve_pct,
 140    )
 141
 142
 143class _DebugLogHandler(logging.Handler):
 144    """Funnels log records into the debug recorder while it's active."""
 145
 146    def __init__(self, recorder) -> None:
 147        super().__init__()
 148        self._recorder = recorder
 149
 150    def emit(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> None:
 151        if not self._recorder.active:
 152            return
 153        try:
 154            self._recorder.write(
 155                "log",
 156                {"level": record.levelname, "name": record.name, "msg": record.getMessage()},
 157                time.time(),
 158            )
 159        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - never let logging crash
 160            pass
 161
 162
 163class _TeeMotor:
 164    """Fan one ``MotorCommand`` out to several motor controllers at once — e.g.
 165    drive the simulated boat AND a real steering servo for bench testing.
 166    Duck-typed to the ``MotorController`` interface (sync ``apply`` + ``flush``,
 167    which may be sync or async)."""
 168
 169    def __init__(self, motors) -> None:
 170        self._motors = [m for m in motors if m is not None]
 171
 172    def apply(self, command) -> None:
 173        for m in self._motors:
 174            m.apply(command)
 175
 176    async def flush(self) -> None:
 177        for m in self._motors:
 178            flush = getattr(m, "flush", None)
 179            if flush is None:
 180                continue
 181            res = flush()
 182            if hasattr(res, "__await__"):
 183                await res
 184
 185
 186class Runtime:
 187    """Owns every component and the background tasks that drive them."""
 188
 189    def __init__(self, config: AppConfig | None = None, *, now_fn=time.time) -> None:
 190        self.config = config or AppConfig()
 191        cfg = self.config
 192
 193        # Injectable monotonic-ish clock so the link-loss failsafe (#64) can be
 194        # driven deterministically in tests instead of by wall-clock.
 195        self._now_fn = now_fn
 196
 197        observability.setup_logging(getattr(cfg, "log_level", "INFO"))
 198        self.bus = EventBus()
 199        observability.wiretap(self.bus)
 200        self.recorder = observability.TelemetryRecorder(ring_size=600)
 201
 202        # Debug session recorder + replay (records telemetry/nmea/commands/logs
 203        # to a gzipped NDJSON file for download + replay).
 204        from .core.debug_recorder import DebugRecorder, ReplayPlayer
 205
 206        self.debug = DebugRecorder(cfg.data_dir)
 207        self.replay = ReplayPlayer()
 208        self.bus.subscribe(events.NMEA_IN, self._record_nmea)
 209        logging.getLogger("vanchor").addHandler(_DebugLogHandler(self.debug))
 210
 211        self.state = NavigationState()
 212        self.state.anchor_radius_m = cfg.control.anchor_radius_m
 213        self.state.max_steer_angle_deg = cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg
 214        self.state.drift_target_knots = cfg.control.drift_default_knots
 215
 216        environment = Environment(
 217            current_speed=cfg.environment.current_speed,
 218            current_dir=cfg.environment.current_dir,
 219            wind_speed=cfg.environment.wind_speed,
 220            wind_dir=cfg.environment.wind_dir,
 221            gust_amplitude_mps=cfg.environment.gust_amplitude_mps,
 222            gust_tau_s=cfg.environment.gust_tau_s,
 223            wind_variability=cfg.environment.wind_variability,
 224            current_variability=cfg.environment.current_variability,
 225        )
 226
 227        # --- devices: simulated and/or real serial hardware (per-device) -- #
 228        # Built via _construct_devices so the SAME logic powers a live reload
 229        # (reload_devices) when the device config changes — no process restart.
 230        self.nmea_tcp = None
 231        self._environment = environment      # reused when devices are rebuilt live
 232        self._sim_task: "asyncio.Task | None" = None
 233        dev = self._construct_devices(cfg)
 234        self.simulator = dev["simulator"]
 235        self.gps = dev["gps"]
 236        self.compass = dev["compass"]
 237        self.depth_sounder = dev["depth_sounder"]
 238        motor = dev["motor"]
 239
 240        # Accumulates depth soundings for the auto depth-map overlay.
 241        self.depth_map = DepthMap()
 242        self._depth_map_path = os.path.join(cfg.data_dir, "depthmap.json")
 243        self._depth_chart_path = os.path.join(cfg.data_dir, "depthchart.json")
 244        self.depth_map.load(self._depth_map_path, self._depth_chart_path)
 245        self._depth_saved_n = len(self.depth_map.points)
 246
 247        # --- navigator + controller (identical for sim or hardware) ------- #
 248        self.navigator = Navigator(
 249            self.state,
 250            self.bus,
 251            SensorGuardConfig(
 252                position_jump_max_m=cfg.sensors.position_jump_max_m,
 253                heading_jump_max_deg=cfg.sensors.heading_jump_max_deg,
 254            ),
 255        )
 256        self.controller = Controller(
 257            self.state,
 258            motor,
 259            self.bus,
 260            tick_hz=cfg.control.tick_hz,
 261            helm=Helm(
 262                PID(
 263                    kp=cfg.control.heading_kp,
 264                    ki=cfg.control.heading_ki,
 265                    kd=cfg.control.heading_kd,
 266                    output_min=-1.0,
 267                    output_max=1.0,
 268                ),
 269                steer_tau=cfg.control.steer_tau,
 270                autopilot_steer_scale=(
 271                    cfg.boat.autopilot_steer_deg / cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg
 272                    if cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg > 0 else 1.0
 273                ),
 274                # Stern mounts yaw the opposite way -> flip the steering sign so
 275                # the autopilot turns the boat the right way.
 276                steer_sign=1.0 if cfg.boat.thruster_x_m() >= 0 else -1.0,
 277                # Pre-cancel the yaw a laterally-offset motor makes under thrust.
 278                thrust_yaw_ff=_thrust_yaw_ff_norm(cfg),
 279            ),
 280            anchor_config=AnchorConfig(
 281                kp=cfg.control.anchor_kp,
 282                kd=cfg.control.anchor_kd,
 283                idle_deadband_m=cfg.control.anchor_idle_deadband_m,
 284                boat_max_speed_mps=cfg.boat.max_speed_mps,
 285            ),
 286            waypoint_config=WaypointConfig(
 287                arrival_radius_m=cfg.control.waypoint_arrival_m,
 288                throttle=cfg.control.waypoint_throttle,
 289                xte_gain=cfg.control.waypoint_xte_gain,
 290            ),
 291            follow_apb_config=FollowApbConfig(throttle=cfg.control.waypoint_throttle),
 292            drift_config=DriftConfig(kp=cfg.control.drift_kp, ki=cfg.control.drift_ki),
 293            safety_config=SafetyConfig(
 294                max_thrust_slew_per_s=cfg.safety.max_thrust_slew_per_s,
 295                # Steering can't rotate faster than the head physically does.
 296                max_steer_slew_per_s=cfg.boat.max_steer_rate_dps / cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg,
 297                reverse_delay_s=cfg.safety.reverse_delay_s,
 298                fix_timeout_s=cfg.safety.fix_timeout_s,
 299                fix_failsafe_enabled=cfg.safety.fix_failsafe_enabled,
 300                drag_alarm_factor=cfg.safety.drag_alarm_factor,
 301                min_depth_m=cfg.safety.min_depth_m,
 302                nogo_lookahead_m=cfg.safety.nogo_lookahead_m,
 303            ),
 304            cruise_pid=PID(
 305                kp=cfg.control.cruise_kp,
 306                ki=cfg.control.cruise_ki,
 307                kd=0.0,
 308                output_min=0.0,
 309                output_max=1.0,
 310            ),
 311            jog_increment_m=cfg.control.jog_increment_m,
 312            track_min_distance_m=cfg.control.track_min_distance_m,
 313        )
 314
 315        if cfg.nmea_tcp.enabled:
 316            from .nav.nmea_net import NmeaTcpServer
 317
 318            self.nmea_tcp = NmeaTcpServer(
 319                self.bus, host=cfg.nmea_tcp.host, port=cfg.nmea_tcp.port
 320            )
 321
 322        # --- Trip log (#66): per-outing track + stats, persisted to disk. - #
 323        self.trip = TripLog(
 324            cfg.data_dir,
 325            min_distance_m=cfg.control.trip_min_distance_m,
 326            auto=cfg.control.auto_trip,
 327            start_speed_kn=cfg.control.trip_start_speed_kn,
 328            idle_timeout_s=cfg.control.trip_idle_timeout_s,
 329        )
 330
 331        self._tasks: list[asyncio.Task] = []
 332        self.calibration = CalibrationRunner(self)
 333
 334        # --- Named boat profiles (#75, #89): persisted, selectable spec bundles.
 335        # On first run (no boats.json) seed a small set of ready-to-pick presets
 336        # with the bow trolling motor active; never clobber a user's saved
 337        # profiles. Then apply whichever profile is marked active so a saved
 338        # selection survives a restart.
 339        from .core.boat_profiles import BoatProfileStore
 340
 341        self.boats = BoatProfileStore(cfg.data_dir)
 342        active = self.boats.active()
 343        if active is not None:
 344            self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 345
 346        # --- Lost-connection failsafe (#64) ------------------------------ #
 347        # Number of connected UI clients and the last time one was seen alive.
 348        self._ui_clients = 0
 349        self._last_client_seen: float | None = None
 350        # True once the failsafe has auto-engaged hold-position (so we don't
 351        # repeatedly re-engage it; cleared on reconnect).
 352        self._link_failsafe_engaged = False
 353        # Route-planning cancellation flag (#54): set by cancel_route_plan(),
 354        # reset at the start of every plan_route() call.
 355        self._route_plan_cancelled = False
 356
 357    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 358    # Boat profile (Init-boat wizard)
 359    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 360    def boat_profile(self) -> dict:
 361        b = self.config.boat
 362        return {
 363            "length_m": b.length_m,
 364            "beam_m": b.beam_m,
 365            "mass_kg": b.mass_kg,
 366            "max_speed_mps": b.max_speed_mps,
 367            "max_thrust_n": b.max_thrust_n,
 368            "thruster_mount": b.thruster_mount,
 369            "thruster_offset_m": b.thruster_offset_m,
 370            "thruster_y_m": b.thruster_y_m,
 371            "thrust_yaw_ff": b.thrust_yaw_ff,
 372            "thrust_yaw_ff_trim": b.thrust_yaw_ff_trim,
 373            "max_steer_angle_deg": b.max_steer_angle_deg,
 374            "max_turn_rate_deg": b.max_turn_rate_deg,
 375            "hull_tracking": b.hull_tracking,
 376            "shaft_dia_mm": b.shaft_dia_mm,
 377            "steer_range_deg": b.steer_range_deg,
 378            "steer_reduction": b.steer_reduction,
 379            "sonar_cone_deg": b.sonar_cone_deg,
 380            # The currently-active named profile (#75) so the UI can highlight it.
 381            "active_boat_id": getattr(self, "boats", None) and self.boats.active_id,
 382        }
 383
 384    def _apply_boat_specs(self, specs: dict) -> None:
 385        """Write a spec dict onto ``config.boat`` and make every live-applicable
 386        field take effect on the running sim/controller.
 387
 388        Numeric/string fields are coerced to ``BoatConfig``'s declared types.
 389        After writing the config we *rebuild* the simulator's physics params via
 390        :func:`_build_boat_params` (not just poke ``max_speed_mps``) so changing
 391        mass, thrust, geometry etc. actually changes the boat's behaviour -- the
 392        Fossen model derives its damping + mass matrices from these at build
 393        time, so an in-place tweak alone would be ignored.
 394        """
 395        b = self.config.boat
 396        for key, val in specs.items():
 397            if hasattr(b, key) and val is not None:
 398                cur = getattr(b, key)
 399                setattr(b, key, type(cur)(val) if isinstance(cur, (int, float)) else val)
 400
 401        # Steering authority / slew limits + hull-character control tuning.
 402        self.state.max_steer_angle_deg = b.max_steer_angle_deg
 403        # The hull character (directional stability) biases the AUTOPILOT TUNING,
 404        # so a boat starts sensibly tuned even on real hardware (where it can't
 405        # change the physics): a stiff, tracking hull (high hull_tracking) resists
 406        # turning -> use MORE steering authority and less command smoothing; a
 407        # loose, skittish hull -> LESS authority and more smoothing to avoid
 408        # hunting. This is a PRIOR -- the auto-calibration drive then measures the
 409        # real boat and refines from here. At hull_tracking == 1.0 it is a no-op.
 410        ht = min(3.0, max(0.25, b.hull_tracking))
 411        if b.max_steer_angle_deg > 0:
 412            self.controller.safety.config.max_steer_slew_per_s = (
 413                b.max_steer_rate_dps / b.max_steer_angle_deg
 414            )
 415            auth_deg = min(b.autopilot_steer_deg * ht, b.max_steer_angle_deg)
 416            self.controller.helm.autopilot_steer_scale = auth_deg / b.max_steer_angle_deg
 417        self.controller.helm.steer_tau = (
 418            self.config.control.steer_tau * min(1.8, max(0.6, ht ** -0.5))
 419        )
 420        # A bow vs stern mount flips which way a steering deflection turns the
 421        # boat -- keep the helm's sign in step so switching profiles never leaves
 422        # the autopilot steering backwards.
 423        self.controller.helm.steer_sign = 1.0 if b.thruster_x_m() >= 0 else -1.0
 424        # Lateral-offset thrust-yaw feed-forward follows the geometry/trim live so
 425        # changing the offset (or the calibrated trim) updates compensation now.
 426        self.controller.helm.thrust_yaw_ff = _thrust_yaw_ff_norm(self.config)
 427        # Anchor mode caps thrust by the boat's top speed; keep it in step.
 428        anchor = self.controller.modes.get(ControlModeName.ANCHOR_HOLD)
 429        if anchor is not None and hasattr(anchor, "config"):
 430            anchor.config.boat_max_speed_mps = b.max_speed_mps
 431        # Waypoint mode's forward/reverse decision needs the boat's measured
 432        # speed, reverse efficiency, and turn rate.
 433        wp = self.controller.modes.get(ControlModeName.WAYPOINT)
 434        if wp is not None and hasattr(wp, "config"):
 435            wp.config.reverse_efficiency = b.reverse_efficiency
 436            wp.config.turn_rate_dps = b.max_turn_rate_deg
 437            wp.config.boat_speed_mps = b.max_speed_mps
 438
 439        # Rebuild the live physics params so mass/thrust/geometry changes bite.
 440        self._rebuild_boat_physics()
 441
 442    def _rebuild_boat_physics(self) -> None:
 443        """Swap the simulator boat's physics params for freshly-built ones.
 444
 445        The Fossen model precomputes its mass + damping matrices (and the
 446        derived surge drag / yaw inertia) in ``__post_init__``/``_build_matrices``
 447        from the params, so we replace ``params`` wholesale and re-derive rather
 448        than mutating fields in place. The simple kinematic model has no derived
 449        state, so swapping the dataclass is enough."""
 450        if self.simulator is None:
 451            return
 452        boat = self.simulator.boat
 453        params = _build_boat_params(self.config)
 454        boat.params = params
 455        # Re-derive the Fossen matrices for the new params (no-op for "simple").
 456        rebuild = getattr(boat, "_build_matrices", None)
 457        if callable(rebuild):
 458            rebuild()
 459
 460    def update_boat(self, fields: dict) -> dict:
 461        """Update the boat profile and apply what can change live.
 462
 463        Also persists the change back into the active named profile (#75) so the
 464        existing ``POST /api/boat`` path and the profile store stay in sync."""
 465        self._apply_boat_specs(fields)
 466        # Write the edited specs back into the active profile so they persist.
 467        if getattr(self, "boats", None) is not None:
 468            from .core.boat_profiles import specs_from_boat
 469
 470            self.boats.save(self.boats.active_id, None, specs_from_boat(self.config.boat))
 471        logger.info("boat profile updated: %s", fields)
 472        return self.boat_profile()
 473
 474    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 475    # Named boat profiles (#75)
 476    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 477    def boat_profiles_list(self) -> dict:
 478        """``{active_id, profiles:[{id,name,...specs}, ...]}``."""
 479        return self.boats.to_dict()
 480
 481    def boat_profiles_create(self, name: str, specs: dict | None = None) -> dict:
 482        """Create a profile (specs default to the current active boat). Returns
 483        ``{id, ...}`` of the new profile."""
 484        from .core.boat_profiles import specs_from_boat
 485
 486        if specs is None:
 487            specs = specs_from_boat(self.config.boat)
 488        pid = self.boats.create(name, specs)
 489        return self.boats.get(pid) or {"id": pid}
 490
 491    def boat_profiles_update(
 492        self, profile_id: str, name: str | None = None, specs: dict | None = None
 493    ) -> dict | None:
 494        """Update a profile's name/specs. If the edited profile is the active
 495        one, also apply the new specs live. Returns the updated profile or None
 496        if the id is unknown."""
 497        if not self.boats.save(profile_id, name, specs):
 498            return None
 499        if profile_id == self.boats.active_id:
 500            active = self.boats.active()
 501            if active is not None:
 502                self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 503        return self.boats.get(profile_id)
 504
 505    def boat_profiles_activate(self, profile_id: str) -> dict | None:
 506        """Make a profile active and apply its specs to the live sim. Returns
 507        the applied boat profile dict, or None if the id is unknown."""
 508        if not self.boats.set_active(profile_id):
 509            return None
 510        active = self.boats.active()
 511        if active is not None:
 512            self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 513        logger.info("activated boat profile %s", profile_id)
 514        return self.boat_profile()
 515
 516    def boat_profiles_delete(self, profile_id: str) -> bool:
 517        """Delete a profile (refuses the last one). If the deleted profile was
 518        active, apply whatever profile is active afterwards."""
 519        if not self.boats.delete(profile_id):
 520            return False
 521        active = self.boats.active()
 522        if active is not None:
 523            self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 524        return True
 525
 526    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 527    # Versioned backup / restore of all persistent state
 528    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 529    def create_backup(self, client: dict | None = None, *, created_at: str | None = None) -> bytes:
 530        """Build a versioned backup ZIP of this runtime's ``data_dir`` (boats,
 531        depth map, devices, trips) plus the UI's ``client`` localStorage slice.
 532
 533        ``created_at`` is an ISO8601 string the caller supplies (the endpoint
 534        passes the request time); when omitted we use the injected clock to make
 535        a UTC timestamp -- the backup module itself never calls ``datetime.now``.
 536        Returns the raw ``.zip`` bytes."""
 537        from .core import backup
 538
 539        if created_at is None:
 540            created_at = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime(self._now_fn()))
 541        return backup.create_backup(
 542            self.config.data_dir, client=client, created_at=created_at
 543        )
 544
 545    def restore_backup(self, zip_bytes: bytes) -> dict:
 546        """Restore a backup ZIP into ``data_dir`` and reload what it can LIVE.
 547
 548        Extracts the archive (overwriting the on-disk files), then refreshes the
 549        in-memory state it can without a restart: re-loads the boat profiles +
 550        the depth map from disk and re-applies the active profile, and reloads
 551        the device config. Anything that can't be refreshed live sets
 552        ``restart_required``. Returns the backup-module result dict plus
 553        ``restart_required``. Raises :class:`ValueError` (-> 400) on a bad zip."""
 554        from .core import backup
 555
 556        result = backup.restore_backup(self.config.data_dir, zip_bytes)
 557        restart_required = False
 558
 559        # Boat profiles: rebuild the store from the restored boats.json and
 560        # re-apply the active profile so the live physics follow it.
 561        try:
 562            from .core.boat_profiles import BoatProfileStore
 563
 564            self.boats = BoatProfileStore(self.config.data_dir)
 565            active = self.boats.active()
 566            if active is not None:
 567                self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 568        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
 569            logger.exception("restore: reloading boat profiles failed")
 570            restart_required = True
 571
 572        # Depth map: reload the restored soundings from disk.
 573        try:
 574            self.depth_map = DepthMap()
 575            self.depth_map.load(self._depth_map_path, self._depth_chart_path)
 576            self._depth_saved_n = len(self.depth_map.points)
 577        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
 578            logger.exception("restore: reloading depth map failed")
 579            restart_required = True
 580
 581        # Device config: re-read the restored devices.json into the live config
 582        # and rebuild the device set (no restart). reload_devices is async, so
 583        # schedule it; if there's no running loop, defer to a restart.
 584        apply_device_overrides(self.config)
 585        try:
 586            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
 587            loop.create_task(self.reload_devices())
 588        except RuntimeError:
 589            # No event loop (e.g. a synchronous restore in a test) -> the new
 590            # device config will take effect on the next start/restart.
 591            restart_required = True
 592
 593        result["restart_required"] = restart_required
 594        logger.info("backup restored (restart_required=%s)", restart_required)
 595        return result
 596
 597    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 598    # Device / hardware config (persisted, editable over the API)
 599    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 600    # The valid values for each source field (used to validate edits + to tell
 601    # the UI which options to offer). Sensors share one set; the motor adds
 602    # "both" (drive the sim boat AND mirror to a real servo).
 603    _SENSOR_SOURCES = ("sim", "serial", "nmea")
 604    _MOTOR_SOURCES = ("sim", "serial", "both")
 605
 606    def _compass_sources(self) -> tuple:
 607        """Built-in compass sources + any registered driver sources (e.g.
 608        ``hwt901b``). Registered drivers are discovered from the plugin registry,
 609        so a new compass driver adds itself here without editing this file."""
 610        from .hardware import registry
 611        return self._SENSOR_SOURCES + tuple(registry.sources("compass"))
 612
 613    def device_config(self) -> dict:
 614        """Current device/hardware config + the selectable options.
 615
 616        Shape matches what :meth:`set_device_config` persists, plus ``options``
 617        (for the UI's selects) and ``restart_required`` (always ``False`` on a
 618        plain read; a POST returns ``True`` because devices are rebuilt only on
 619        restart, not hot-swapped)."""
 620        return {
 621            "hardware": asdict(self.config.hardware),
 622            "nmea_tcp": asdict(self.config.nmea_tcp),
 623            "options": {
 624                "sensor": list(self._SENSOR_SOURCES),
 625                "compass": list(self._compass_sources()),
 626                "motor": list(self._MOTOR_SOURCES),
 627            },
 628            "menus": self._device_menus(),
 629            "driver_menus": self._driver_menus(),
 630            "restart_required": False,
 631        }
 632
 633    def set_device_config(self, payload: dict) -> dict:
 634        """Validate, persist, and apply a device-config edit.
 635
 636        ``payload`` is ``{"hardware": {...}, "nmea_tcp": {...}}`` (either key
 637        optional). Validates source values + field types, writes
 638        ``devices.json``, and updates the in-memory ``config.hardware`` /
 639        ``config.nmea_tcp`` so a subsequent read reflects it. Devices are NOT
 640        hot-swapped; the change applies on the next restart, so the returned
 641        ``restart_required`` is ``True``. Raises :class:`ValueError` on a bad
 642        payload (the endpoint maps it to a 400)."""
 643        if not isinstance(payload, dict):
 644            raise ValueError("payload must be an object")
 645        hw_in = payload.get("hardware") or {}
 646        nmea_in = payload.get("nmea_tcp") or {}
 647        if not isinstance(hw_in, dict) or not isinstance(nmea_in, dict):
 648            raise ValueError("'hardware' and 'nmea_tcp' must be objects")
 649
 650        # Build validated copies off the *current* config (so an edit can be
 651        # partial). Sources: sensors sim|serial|nmea, motor sim|serial|both.
 652        hw = HardwareConfig(**asdict(self.config.hardware))
 653        for dev in ("gps", "compass", "depth"):
 654            key = f"{dev}_source"
 655            allowed = self._compass_sources() if dev == "compass" else self._SENSOR_SOURCES
 656            if hw_in.get(key) is not None and hw_in[key] not in allowed:
 657                raise ValueError(
 658                    f"{key} must be one of {allowed} (got {hw_in[key]!r})"
 659                )
 660        if hw_in.get("motor_source") is not None and hw_in["motor_source"] not in self._MOTOR_SOURCES:
 661            raise ValueError(
 662                f"motor_source must be one of {self._MOTOR_SOURCES} (got {hw_in['motor_source']!r})"
 663            )
 664        # Ports are strings; baudrate is an int. Coerce/validate via the merge.
 665        for key in ("gps_port", "compass_port", "motor_port"):
 666            if key in hw_in and hw_in[key] is not None and not isinstance(hw_in[key], str):
 667                raise ValueError(f"{key} must be a string")
 668        for key, src in (("baudrate", hw_in), ("port", nmea_in)):
 669            if key in src and src[key] is not None:
 670                try:
 671                    int(src[key])
 672                except (TypeError, ValueError):
 673                    raise ValueError(f"{key} must be an integer") from None
 674
 675        nmea = NmeaTcpConfig(**asdict(self.config.nmea_tcp))
 676        _merge_into(hw, hw_in)
 677        _merge_into(nmea, nmea_in)
 678
 679        save_device_overrides(self.config.data_dir, hw, nmea)
 680        # Reflect the edit in the live config so a subsequent GET shows it
 681        # (the actual devices are rebuilt only on restart).
 682        self.config.hardware = hw
 683        self.config.nmea_tcp = nmea
 684        logger.info("device config updated (restart required to apply): %s", payload)
 685        # Persisted + reflected in-memory; devices are rebuilt on the next start.
 686        return {"ok": True, "restart_required": True}
 687
 688    def _build_serial_gps(self, cfg: AppConfig):
 689        from .hardware.serial_devices import SerialGps
 690        from .hardware.serial_link import PySerialTransport
 691        hw = cfg.hardware
 692        return SerialGps(PySerialTransport(hw.gps_port, baudrate=hw.baudrate), self.bus)
 693
 694    def _device_menus(self) -> list:
 695        """Collect device-specific menus (settings/actions) from the active
 696        devices that expose ``device_menu()`` -- surfaced to the UI so a driver
 697        can offer its own controls (e.g. the HWT901B compass)."""
 698        out: list = []
 699        for dev in (self.gps, self.compass, self.depth_sounder):
 700            fn = getattr(dev, "device_menu", None)
 701            if callable(fn):
 702                try:
 703                    out.append(fn())
 704                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - a bad menu can't break config
 705                    logger.warning("device_menu failed: %s", exc)
 706        return out
 707
 708    def _driver_menus(self) -> dict:
 709        """Per-source device-menu SCHEMAS from registered drivers, with any saved
 710        settings overlaid -- so the UI can render a device's menu the moment its
 711        source is selected, before any instance exists. Keyed by source name."""
 712        out: dict = {}
 713        saved_all = self.config.hardware.device_settings or {}
 714        for kind in ("compass",):  # device kinds with pluggable driver menus
 715            for src, schema in registry.menus(kind).items():
 716                out[src] = _overlay_menu_values(schema, saved_all.get(kind, {}))
 717        return out
 718
 719    def _device_by_kind(self, kind: str):
 720        return {"gps": self.gps, "compass": self.compass,
 721                "depth": self.depth_sounder}.get(kind)
 722
 723    def apply_device_setting(self, kind: str, key: str, value) -> dict:
 724        """Persist a device-menu setting for ``kind`` and apply it live if the
 725        device is running. Persisted settings are read when the device is
 726        (re)built, so a choice sticks even when the device isn't active yet."""
 727        fn = getattr(self._device_by_kind(kind), "apply_setting", None)
 728        known = any(
 729            key in {s.get("key") for s in menu.get("settings", [])}
 730            for menu in registry.menus(kind).values()
 731        )
 732        if not known and not callable(fn):
 733            return {"ok": False, "message": f"no settings for device {kind!r}"}
 734        ds = self.config.hardware.device_settings
 735        ds.setdefault(kind, {})[key] = value
 736        try:
 737            save_device_overrides(self.config.data_dir, self.config.hardware,
 738                                  self.config.nmea_tcp)
 739        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 740            return {"ok": False, "message": f"could not save: {exc}"}
 741        # Apply to the running device too, if there is one that accepts it.
 742        live = fn(key, value) if callable(fn) else None
 743        applied_live = bool(live and live.get("ok"))
 744        return {"ok": True, "saved": True, "applied_live": applied_live,
 745                "restart_required": not applied_live}
 746
 747    def run_device_action(self, kind: str, name: str, params: dict | None = None) -> dict:
 748        """Run a device-menu action on the active device of ``kind``."""
 749        fn = getattr(self._device_by_kind(kind), "run_action", None)
 750        if not callable(fn):
 751            return {"ok": False, "message": f"no actions for device {kind!r}"}
 752        try:
 753            return fn(name, params or {})
 754        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 755            return {"ok": False, "message": str(exc)}
 756
 757    def _build_serial_compass(self, cfg: AppConfig):
 758        from .hardware.serial_devices import SerialCompass
 759        from .hardware.serial_link import PySerialTransport
 760        hw = cfg.hardware
 761        return SerialCompass(PySerialTransport(hw.compass_port, baudrate=hw.baudrate), self.bus)
 762
 763    def _build_serial_motor(self, cfg: AppConfig):
 764        from .hardware.serial_devices import SerialMotorController
 765        from .hardware.serial_link import PySerialTransport
 766        hw = cfg.hardware
 767        return SerialMotorController(PySerialTransport(hw.motor_port, baudrate=hw.baudrate))
 768
 769    def _construct_devices(self, cfg: AppConfig) -> dict:
 770        """Build the device set (simulator + sensors + motor) for ``cfg.hardware``.
 771        Returns a dict and does NOT mutate ``self`` — so a live reload can build
 772        the new set first and swap it in only on success (see reload_devices)."""
 773        src = {n: cfg.hardware.source(n) for n in ("gps", "compass", "depth", "motor")}
 774        # The sim boat exists whenever any device is simulated (sensors read its
 775        # truth; the sim motor drives it).
 776        simulator = None
 777        if any(s in ("sim", "both") for s in src.values()):
 778            # On a LIVE reload, carry over the current boat state so a device
 779            # change doesn't teleport the simulated boat back to the start.
 780            prev = getattr(self, "simulator", None)
 781            start_state = (
 782                prev.truth() if prev is not None
 783                else BoatState(point=GeoPoint(cfg.sim.start_lat, cfg.sim.start_lon), heading_deg=0.0)
 784            )
 785            simulator = Simulator(
 786                start=start_state,
 787                params=_build_boat_params(cfg),
 788                environment=self._environment,
 789                physics_hz=cfg.sim.physics_hz,
 790                time_scale=cfg.sim.time_scale,
 791                model=cfg.sim.model,
 792                battery_config=_build_battery_config(cfg),
 793            )
 794        sim_motor = simulator.motor if simulator is not None else None
 795        if src["motor"] == "serial":
 796            motor = self._build_serial_motor(cfg)
 797        elif src["motor"] == "both":
 798            motor = _TeeMotor([sim_motor, self._build_serial_motor(cfg)])
 799        else:
 800            motor = sim_motor
 801        # "nmea" (or anything not sim/serial) builds NO internal sensor: the
 802        # navigator is fed by external NMEA over the bridge/inject instead.
 803        gps = compass = depth = None
 804        if src["gps"] == "serial":
 805            gps = self._build_serial_gps(cfg)
 806        elif src["gps"] == "sim":
 807            gps = SimGps(simulator.truth, self.bus, update_hz=cfg.sensors.gps_hz,
 808                         position_noise_m=cfg.sensors.gps_noise_m)
 809        if src["compass"] == "serial":
 810            compass = self._build_serial_compass(cfg)
 811        elif src["compass"] == "sim":
 812            compass = SimCompass(simulator.truth, self.bus, update_hz=cfg.sensors.compass_hz,
 813                                 heading_noise_deg=cfg.sensors.compass_noise_deg)
 814        elif registry.has("compass", src["compass"]):
 815            # A pluggable driver builds eagerly (may open a port / import an
 816            # optional lib), so a failure here must NOT crash startup -- skip it,
 817            # log why, and leave the UI reachable to fix the config (mirrors the
 818            # serial "unopenable device" resilience). The warning shows in
 819            # Settings -> View logs.
 820            try:
 821                compass = registry.build_device("compass", src["compass"], self, cfg)
 822            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 823                logger.warning(
 824                    "compass source %r could not be built (%s); running without a "
 825                    "compass. Change it in Settings -> Devices.", src["compass"], exc)
 826                compass = None
 827        if src["depth"] == "sim":
 828            depth = SimDepthSounder(
 829                simulator.truth,
 830                Bathymetry(origin=GeoPoint(cfg.sim.start_lat, cfg.sim.start_lon)),
 831                self.bus, update_hz=cfg.sensors.depth_hz)
 832        return {"simulator": simulator, "gps": gps, "compass": compass,
 833                "depth_sounder": depth, "motor": motor}
 834
 835    async def reload_devices(self) -> dict:
 836        """Rebuild the device set LIVE (no process restart) so a device-config
 837        change applies immediately. Builds + starts the NEW set first, and only
 838        stops the old + swaps in if that succeeds — so a bad serial port leaves
 839        the current devices running and the autopilot uninterrupted. Returns
 840        ``{applied: bool, error?: str}``."""
 841        try:
 842            new = self._construct_devices(self.config)
 843        except Exception as exc:  # e.g. a serial port that doesn't exist
 844            logger.exception("device reload: build failed")
 845            return {"applied": False, "error": str(exc)}
 846        started: list = []
 847        try:
 848            for d in (new["gps"], new["compass"], new["depth_sounder"]):
 849                if d is not None:
 850                    await d.start()
 851                    started.append(d)
 852            new_sim_task = (asyncio.ensure_future(new["simulator"].run())
 853                            if new["simulator"] is not None else None)
 854        except Exception as exc:
 855            logger.exception("device reload: start failed; rolling back")
 856            for d in started:
 857                with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
 858                    await d.stop()
 859            return {"applied": False, "error": str(exc)}
 860        # New set is live — now retire the old one and swap references.
 861        for d in (self.gps, self.compass, self.depth_sounder):
 862            if d is not None:
 863                with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
 864                    await d.stop()
 865        if self.simulator is not None:
 866            self.simulator.stop()
 867        if self._sim_task is not None:
 868            self._sim_task.cancel()
 869        self.gps, self.compass, self.depth_sounder = new["gps"], new["compass"], new["depth_sounder"]
 870        self.simulator, self._sim_task = new["simulator"], new_sim_task
 871        self.controller.motor = new["motor"]
 872        # Re-prime the navigator with a fresh fix/heading so the fix-lost failsafe
 873        # doesn't latch (and stop the motor) over the brief gap during the swap.
 874        for dev in (self.gps, self.compass):
 875            if dev is not None:
 876                with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
 877                    self.navigator.handle_sentence(dev.sample())
 878        logger.info("device config applied live")
 879        return {"applied": True}
 880
 881    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 882    # Commands (from the UI).
 883    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 884    async def _record_nmea(self, sentence: str) -> None:
 885        if self.debug.active:
 886            self.debug.write("nmea", sentence, time.time())
 887
 888    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 889    # Debug session recording + replay
 890    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 891    def start_debug(self, name: str | None = None) -> dict:
 892        nm = name or "session-" + time.strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S", time.localtime(time.time()))
 893        return self.debug.start(nm, time.time())
 894
 895    def stop_debug(self) -> dict:
 896        return self.debug.stop()
 897
 898    def start_replay(self, file_name: str) -> bool:
 899        path = self.debug.path_for(file_name)
 900        if path is None:
 901            return False
 902        return self.replay.load(path, time.time())
 903
 904    def stop_replay(self) -> None:
 905        self.replay.stop()
 906
 907    def handle_command(self, command: dict) -> None:
 908        ctype = command.get("type")
 909        if self.debug.active and ctype not in (None,):
 910            self.debug.write("command", command, time.time())
 911        if ctype == "set_environment" and self.simulator is not None:
 912            env = self.simulator.environment
 913            for key in (
 914                "current_speed",
 915                "current_dir",
 916                "wind_speed",
 917                "wind_dir",
 918                "gust_amplitude_mps",
 919                "wind_variability",
 920                "current_variability",
 921            ):
 922                if key in command:
 923                    setattr(env, key, float(command[key]))
 924            # Re-anchor the slow weather wander on the new base values.
 925            self.simulator.set_weather_base()
 926        elif ctype == "weather_preset" and self.simulator is not None:
 927            self._apply_weather_preset(str(command.get("id", "")))
 928        elif ctype == "teleport":
 929            self._teleport(command)
 930        elif ctype == "inject_nmea":
 931            asyncio.ensure_future(self.bus.publish(events.NMEA_IN, str(command["sentence"])))
 932        elif ctype == "set_gps_offset":
 933            self.navigator.set_gps_offset(
 934                float(command["true_lat"]), float(command["true_lon"])
 935            )
 936        elif ctype == "clear_gps_offset":
 937            self.navigator.clear_gps_offset()
 938        elif ctype == "load_route":
 939            self._load_route(command)
 940        elif ctype == "set_battery":
 941            self._set_battery(command.get("soc_pct"))
 942        elif ctype == "return_to_launch":
 943            self.return_to_launch()
 944        elif ctype == "trip_start":
 945            self.trip_start(command.get("name"))
 946        elif ctype == "trip_stop":
 947            self.trip_stop()
 948        else:
 949            self.controller.handle_command(command)
 950
 951    def _teleport(self, command: dict) -> None:
 952        """Sim teleport (#90): instantly snap the simulated boat's ground truth to
 953        a new ``lat``/``lon`` (and optional ``heading``), zeroing its velocity so
 954        it doesn't keep coasting. A safe no-op on real hardware (no simulator)."""
 955        if self.simulator is None:
 956            logger.info("teleport ignored (no simulator)")
 957            return
 958        heading = command.get("heading")
 959        self.simulator.teleport(
 960            float(command["lat"]),
 961            float(command["lon"]),
 962            float(heading) if heading is not None else None,
 963        )
 964        # Re-prime the GPS spike-guard so the next fix at the new spot snaps
 965        # straight through instead of being rejected as a position jump.
 966        guard = self.navigator.guard
 967        guard._last_point = None
 968        guard._pending_point = None
 969
 970    def _set_battery(self, soc_pct: object) -> None:
 971        """Set/reset the battery state-of-charge (#60). Sim-only: on real
 972        hardware the SOC comes from a battery monitor over the HAL."""
 973        if soc_pct is None or self.simulator is None:
 974            logger.info("set_battery ignored (no value or no sim battery)")
 975            return
 976        self.simulator.battery.set_soc(float(soc_pct))
 977        logger.info("battery SOC set to %.0f%%", float(soc_pct))
 978
 979    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 980    # Return-to-Launch (#61)
 981    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 982    def return_to_launch(self) -> dict:
 983        """Plan a water route from the boat to its launch point and follow it,
 984        anchoring on arrival.
 985
 986        Returns the plan result dict. Synchronous + CPU/IO-heavy (water fetch +
 987        routing); call it from an executor on the live path.
 988        """
 989        launch = self.state.launch
 990        if launch is None:
 991            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": "No launch point set."}
 992        result = self.plan_route(launch.lat, launch.lon, mode="fastest")
 993        if not result.get("ok") or not result.get("waypoints"):
 994            return result
 995        self.state.waypoints = [
 996            Waypoint(name=str(w.get("name", "WP")), point=GeoPoint(w["lat"], w["lon"]))
 997            for w in result["waypoints"]
 998        ]
 999        self.controller.handle_command({"type": "load_route", "on_arrival": "anchor"})
1000        # load_route doesn't take on_arrival; set it explicitly on the state.
1001        self.state.route_on_arrival = "anchor"
1002        logger.info("Return-to-Launch engaged: %d waypoints home", len(self.state.waypoints))
1003        return result
1004
1005    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1006    # Trip log (#66)
1007    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1008    def trip_start(self, name: str | None = None) -> dict:
1009        """Manually start a trip (overrides/replaces any active one)."""
1010        trip = self.trip.start(name, self._now_fn())
1011        return self.trip.snapshot(self._now_fn())
1012
1013    def trip_stop(self) -> dict:
1014        """Manually stop + persist the active trip. No-op when none is active."""
1015        self.trip.stop(self._now_fn())
1016        return self.trip.snapshot(self._now_fn())
1017
1018    def trip_list(self) -> list[dict]:
1019        return self.trip.list_trips()
1020
1021    def trip_get(self, trip_id: str) -> dict | None:
1022        return self.trip.get_trip(trip_id)
1023
1024    def trip_gpx(self, trip_id: str) -> str | None:
1025        return self.trip.gpx(trip_id)
1026
1027    def trip_delete(self, trip_id: str) -> bool:
1028        return self.trip.delete_trip(trip_id)
1029
1030    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1031    # Battery (#60)
1032    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1033    def battery_snapshot(self) -> dict:
1034        """Battery telemetry. From the sim battery, or zeros if none (hardware
1035        battery monitor over the HAL will populate this later)."""
1036        if self.simulator is not None:
1037            return self.simulator.battery.to_dict()
1038        return {
1039            "soc_pct": 0.0,
1040            "voltage_v": 0.0,
1041            "current_a": 0.0,
1042            "draw_w": 0.0,
1043            "range_m": 0.0,
1044            "time_to_empty_s": None,
1045        }
1046
1047    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1048    # Lost-connection failsafe (#64) + RTL auto-recommend (#61)
1049    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1050    def client_connected(self) -> None:
1051        """A UI client connected; clear any link failsafe."""
1052        self._ui_clients += 1
1053        self._last_client_seen = self._now_fn()
1054        if self._link_failsafe_engaged:
1055            logger.info("UI client reconnected; link failsafe cleared")
1056        self._link_failsafe_engaged = False
1057
1058    def client_activity(self) -> None:
1059        """Mark the link alive (any inbound client traffic)."""
1060        self._last_client_seen = self._now_fn()
1061
1062    def client_disconnected(self) -> None:
1063        """A UI client disconnected."""
1064        self._ui_clients = max(0, self._ui_clients - 1)
1065        self._last_client_seen = self._now_fn()
1066
1067    def _underway(self) -> bool:
1068        """True when the boat is in a guided/cruising mode actively making way --
1069        i.e. not already idle-manual or holding station on anchor."""
1070        return self.state.mode in _UNDERWAY_MODES
1071
1072    def evaluate_link_failsafe(self, now: float | None = None) -> bool:
1073        """Engage hold-position if no UI client has been seen for the timeout
1074        while underway. Returns True if it engaged on this call. Idempotent and
1075        clock-injectable (pass ``now`` in tests)."""
1076        if now is None:
1077            now = self._now_fn()
1078        timeout = self.config.safety.link_loss_timeout_s
1079        connected = self._ui_clients > 0
1080        if connected or self._last_client_seen is None or self._link_failsafe_engaged:
1081            return False
1082        if not self._underway():
1083            return False
1084        if now - self._last_client_seen < timeout:
1085            return False
1086        # Lost the link while underway -> hold position (anchor-hold here).
1087        logger.warning("link lost %.0fs while underway; engaging hold-position", timeout)
1088        self.controller.handle_command({"type": "anchor_hold"})
1089        self._link_failsafe_engaged = True
1090        return True
1091
1092    def evaluate_rtl_recommend(self) -> bool:
1093        """Set ``state.rtl_recommended`` when the battery range has dropped to
1094        within ``rtl_margin_m`` of the distance home (so the boat can *just* make
1095        it back). If ``auto_rtl`` is set, engage RTL. Returns the new flag."""
1096        launch = self.state.launch
1097        pos = self.state.position
1098        if launch is None or pos is None or pos.is_null():
1099            self.state.rtl_recommended = False
1100            return False
1101        range_m = self.battery_snapshot().get("range_m", 0.0)
1102        if range_m <= 0.0:
1103            # No usable range estimate yet (boat not making way) -> don't prompt.
1104            self.state.rtl_recommended = False
1105            return False
1106        from .core.geo import haversine_m
1107
1108        dist_home = haversine_m(pos, launch)
1109        recommend = range_m <= dist_home + self.config.safety.rtl_margin_m
1110        self.state.rtl_recommended = recommend
1111        if recommend and self.config.safety.auto_rtl and self.state.mode != ControlModeName.WAYPOINT:
1112            logger.warning("auto_rtl: battery range near distance-home; engaging RTL")
1113            self.return_to_launch()
1114        return recommend
1115
1116    def _load_route(self, command: dict) -> None:
1117        from .nav.routes import parse_gpx
1118
1119        try:
1120            if "gpx" in command:
1121                text = str(command["gpx"])
1122            elif "path" in command:
1123                with open(command["path"], "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1124                    text = fh.read()
1125            else:
1126                logger.warning("load_route requires 'gpx' or 'path'")
1127                return
1128            waypoints = parse_gpx(text)
1129        except (ValueError, OSError) as exc:
1130            logger.warning("load_route failed: %s", exc)
1131            return
1132        self.state.waypoints = waypoints
1133        self.controller.handle_command(
1134            {"type": "load_route", "throttle": command.get("throttle")}
1135            if command.get("throttle") is not None
1136            else {"type": "load_route"}
1137        )
1138        logger.info("loaded route with %d waypoints", len(waypoints))
1139
1140    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1141    # Smart "Take me here" water routing (task #43)
1142    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1143    def plan_route(
1144        self, dest_lat: float, dest_lon: float, mode: str = "fastest", offset_m: float = 25.0
1145    ) -> dict:
1146        """Plan a water-only route from the boat's current position.
1147
1148        Synchronous and CPU/IO-heavy (Overpass fetch + shapely/networkx); the UI
1149        endpoint calls it in an executor. Returns the API contract dict. Does NOT
1150        start navigation.
1151        """
1152        from .nav import routing, water
1153
1154        # Fresh plan: clear any stale cancel request so a normal plan runs.
1155        self._route_plan_cancelled = False
1156        pos = self.state.position
1157        if pos is not None and not pos.is_null():
1158            start_lat, start_lon = pos.lat, pos.lon
1159        else:
1160            start_lat, start_lon = self.config.sim.start_lat, self.config.sim.start_lon
1161
1162        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1163        bbox = water.bbox_around(start_lat, start_lon, dest_lat, dest_lon)
1164        water_ll = cache.find_covering(bbox)
1165        if water_ll is None:
1166            try:
1167                elements = water.fetch_overpass(*bbox)
1168            except Exception as exc:  # network / endpoint failure
1169                logger.warning("water fetch failed: %s", exc)
1170                return {
1171                    "ok": False,
1172                    "waypoints": [],
1173                    "message": "No offline chart for this area; connect once to download it.",
1174                }
1175            water_ll = water.assemble_water(elements)
1176            if water_ll.is_empty:
1177                return {
1178                    "ok": False,
1179                    "waypoints": [],
1180                    "message": "No mapped water found around the route.",
1181                }
1182            try:
1183                cache.store(bbox, water_ll)
1184            except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1185                logger.warning("water cache store failed: %s", exc)
1186
1187        result = routing.plan_route(
1188            start_lat=start_lat,
1189            start_lon=start_lon,
1190            dest_lat=dest_lat,
1191            dest_lon=dest_lon,
1192            water_ll=water_ll,
1193            mode=mode,
1194            shoreline_offset_m=offset_m,
1195            cancelled=lambda: self._route_plan_cancelled,
1196        )
1197        return {
1198            "ok": result.ok,
1199            "waypoints": result.waypoints,
1200            "message": result.message,
1201        }
1202
1203    def cancel_route_plan(self) -> None:
1204        """Request that an in-progress route plan abort ASAP (#54)."""
1205        self._route_plan_cancelled = True
1206
1207    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1208    # "Around island" loop route (#77)
1209    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1210    def plan_island_loop(
1211        self, click_lat: float, click_lon: float, offset_m: float = 20.0
1212    ) -> dict:
1213        """Plan a closed loop route encircling the island under ``(lat, lon)``.
1214
1215        Uses the same offline water chart/cache as :meth:`plan_route` (fetches
1216        once if not cached). The boat's current position (or the sim start)
1217        decides which water body is the basin. Does NOT start navigation -- it
1218        returns waypoints for the route editor. Synchronous + CPU/IO-heavy; the
1219        UI endpoint calls it in an executor. Returns
1220        ``{ok, waypoints, loop, message}``.
1221        """
1222        from .nav import routing, water
1223
1224        pos = self.state.position
1225        if pos is not None and not pos.is_null():
1226            boat_lat, boat_lon = pos.lat, pos.lon
1227        else:
1228            boat_lat, boat_lon = self.config.sim.start_lat, self.config.sim.start_lon
1229
1230        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1231        bbox = water.bbox_around(boat_lat, boat_lon, click_lat, click_lon)
1232        water_ll = cache.find_covering(bbox)
1233        if water_ll is None:
1234            try:
1235                elements = water.fetch_overpass(*bbox)
1236            except Exception as exc:  # network / endpoint failure
1237                logger.warning("water fetch failed: %s", exc)
1238                return {
1239                    "ok": False,
1240                    "waypoints": [],
1241                    "loop": True,
1242                    "message": "No offline chart for this area; connect once to download it.",
1243                }
1244            water_ll = water.assemble_water(elements)
1245            if water_ll.is_empty:
1246                return {
1247                    "ok": False,
1248                    "waypoints": [],
1249                    "loop": True,
1250                    "message": "No mapped water found around the island.",
1251                }
1252            try:
1253                cache.store(bbox, water_ll)
1254            except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1255                logger.warning("water cache store failed: %s", exc)
1256
1257        result = routing.plan_island_loop(
1258            click_lat,
1259            click_lon,
1260            water_ll,
1261            boat_lat=boat_lat,
1262            boat_lon=boat_lon,
1263            offset_m=offset_m,
1264        )
1265        return {
1266            "ok": result.ok,
1267            "waypoints": result.waypoints,
1268            "loop": result.loop,
1269            "message": result.message,
1270        }
1271
1272    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1273    # Area survey "map mode" route (#47)
1274    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1275    def plan_survey(
1276        self, polygon_latlon: list, spacing_m: float, angle_deg: float | None = None
1277    ) -> dict:
1278        """Plan a boustrophedon coverage route over a closed area polygon.
1279
1280        Pure CPU work (shapely); the UI endpoint calls it in an executor. Does
1281        NOT start navigation -- it returns waypoints for the route editor.
1282        """
1283        from .nav import survey
1284
1285        try:
1286            result = survey.plan_survey(polygon_latlon, float(spacing_m), angle_deg)
1287        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
1288            logger.warning("survey plan failed: %s", exc)
1289            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": f"Bad survey request: {exc}"}
1290        return {
1291            "ok": result.ok,
1292            "waypoints": result.waypoints,
1293            "message": result.message,
1294        }
1295
1296    def plan_work_spots(self, polygon_latlon: list, spacing_m: float) -> dict:
1297        """Generate Work Area spots: an even serpentine grid over a drawn area,
1298        clipped to water (spots on land are dropped). Pure CPU (shapely) + the
1299        offline water cache; the UI endpoint calls it in an executor. Returns
1300        ``{ok, waypoints, message}`` -- the UI loads these as the Work Area spots."""
1301        from .nav import survey, water as water_mod
1302        from shapely.geometry import Point as _Pt
1303
1304        try:
1305            result = survey.plan_work_spots(polygon_latlon, float(spacing_m))
1306        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
1307            logger.warning("work-area spots failed: %s", exc)
1308            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": f"Bad work-area request: {exc}"}
1309        if not result.ok:
1310            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": result.message}
1311
1312        # Clip spots to water (drop any over land) using the cached water polygon
1313        # for the area's bbox; if no water is cached, return the grid unclipped.
1314        wps = result.waypoints
1315        try:
1316            lats = [w["lat"] for w in wps]
1317            lons = [w["lon"] for w in wps]
1318            cache = water_mod.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1319            bbox = water_mod.bbox_around(min(lats), min(lons), max(lats), max(lons))
1320            geom = cache.find_covering(bbox)
1321            if geom is not None and not geom.is_empty:
1322                proj = water_mod.Projection.for_point(
1323                    (min(lons) + max(lons)) / 2, (min(lats) + max(lats)) / 2
1324                )
1325                water_m = proj.to_metric(geom)
1326                if not water_m.is_valid:
1327                    water_m = water_m.buffer(0)
1328                kept = [
1329                    w for w in wps
1330                    if water_m.covers(_Pt(*proj.point_to_metric(w["lon"], w["lat"])))
1331                ]
1332                if kept:  # only apply the clip if something survives (else keep all)
1333                    wps = [dict(w, name=f"Spot {i + 1}") for i, w in enumerate(kept)]
1334        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - clipping is best-effort
1335            logger.warning("work-area water clip skipped: %s", exc)
1336        return {"ok": True, "waypoints": wps,
1337                "message": f"{len(wps)} work spots." if wps else result.message}
1338
1339    def contour_route(self, lat: float, lon: float, window_m: float = 700.0) -> dict:
1340        """Build a route that follows the imported depth contour nearest
1341        (lat, lon), chaining same-depth pieces into a continuous track (a closed
1342        isobath comes back as a loop). Pure CPU (shapely); the UI endpoint calls it
1343        in an executor. Returns ``{ok, waypoints, depth_m, loop, message}`` -- the
1344        UI loads the waypoints as a route (patrol optional)."""
1345        from .nav import contour_route as cr
1346
1347        dlat = window_m / 111_320.0
1348        dlon = window_m / (111_320.0 * max(0.1, math.cos(math.radians(lat))))
1349        bbox = (lon - dlon, lat - dlat, lon + dlon, lat + dlat)  # (w, s, e, n)
1350        contours = self.depth_map.contours_in(bbox=bbox)
1351        if not contours:
1352            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [],
1353                    "message": "No depth contours loaded around there."}
1354        return cr.contour_route_near(lat, lon, contours)
1355
1356    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1357    # Offline chart prefetch + management (#52)
1358    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1359    def prefetch_chart(self, bbox: list) -> dict:
1360        """Fetch + cache the water polygon for a bbox so the boat can route
1361        offline later. ``bbox`` is ``[south, west, north, east]``.
1362
1363        Synchronous and IO-heavy (Overpass fetch); call it in an executor.
1364        Handles network failure gracefully.
1365        """
1366        from .nav import water
1367
1368        try:
1369            south, west, north, east = (float(v) for v in bbox)
1370        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1371            return {
1372                "ok": False,
1373                "cached": False,
1374                "vertices": 0,
1375                "message": "bbox must be [south, west, north, east].",
1376            }
1377        box = (south, west, north, east)
1378        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1379        existing = cache.find_covering(box)
1380        if existing is not None:
1381            return {
1382                "ok": True,
1383                "cached": True,
1384                "vertices": water._count_vertices(existing),
1385                "message": "Area already cached.",
1386            }
1387        try:
1388            elements = water.fetch_overpass(*box)
1389        except Exception as exc:  # network / endpoint failure
1390            logger.warning("chart prefetch fetch failed: %s", exc)
1391            return {
1392                "ok": False,
1393                "cached": False,
1394                "vertices": 0,
1395                "message": f"Could not download chart (offline?): {exc}",
1396            }
1397        water_ll = water.assemble_water(elements)
1398        if water_ll.is_empty:
1399            return {
1400                "ok": False,
1401                "cached": False,
1402                "vertices": 0,
1403                "message": "No mapped water found in that area.",
1404            }
1405        try:
1406            cache.store(box, water_ll)
1407        except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1408            logger.warning("chart cache store failed: %s", exc)
1409            return {
1410                "ok": False,
1411                "cached": False,
1412                "vertices": water._count_vertices(water_ll),
1413                "message": f"Fetched chart but failed to cache it: {exc}",
1414            }
1415        return {
1416            "ok": True,
1417            "cached": True,
1418            "vertices": water._count_vertices(water_ll),
1419            "message": "Chart downloaded and cached for offline routing.",
1420        }
1421
1422    def list_charts(self) -> dict:
1423        """List cached chart bboxes + on-disk sizes (for the UI to show/manage)."""
1424        import json as _json
1425
1426        from .nav import water
1427
1428        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1429        charts: list[dict] = []
1430        if cache.dir.exists():
1431            for meta_path in sorted(cache.dir.glob("*.json")):
1432                try:
1433                    meta = _json.loads(meta_path.read_text())
1434                    bbox = meta["bbox"]
1435                except (OSError, ValueError, KeyError):
1436                    continue
1437                wkb_path = meta_path.with_suffix(".wkb")
1438                size = wkb_path.stat().st_size if wkb_path.exists() else 0
1439                charts.append(
1440                    {
1441                        "bbox": bbox,
1442                        "vertices": meta.get("vertices", 0),
1443                        "size_bytes": size,
1444                    }
1445                )
1446        return {"charts": charts}
1447
1448    def clear_charts(self) -> dict:
1449        """Delete every cached chart. Returns how many were removed."""
1450        from .nav import water
1451
1452        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1453        removed = 0
1454        if cache.dir.exists():
1455            for path in list(cache.dir.glob("*.wkb")) + list(cache.dir.glob("*.json")):
1456                try:
1457                    path.unlink()
1458                    if path.suffix == ".wkb":
1459                        removed += 1
1460                except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1461                    logger.warning("could not remove cached chart %s: %s", path, exc)
1462        return {"ok": True, "removed": removed, "message": f"Cleared {removed} cached chart(s)."}
1463
1464    def _apply_weather_preset(self, preset_id: str) -> None:
1465        """Apply a named weather preset to the live sim environment."""
1466        from .sim.weather import WEATHER_PRESETS
1467
1468        preset = WEATHER_PRESETS.get(preset_id)
1469        if preset is None or self.simulator is None:
1470            logger.warning("unknown weather preset: %r", preset_id)
1471            return
1472        env = self.simulator.environment
1473        env.current_speed = preset.current_speed
1474        env.current_dir = preset.current_dir
1475        env.wind_speed = preset.wind_speed
1476        env.wind_dir = preset.wind_dir
1477        env.gust_amplitude_mps = preset.gust_amplitude_mps
1478        env.wind_variability = preset.wind_variability
1479        env.current_variability = preset.current_variability
1480        self.simulator.set_weather_base()
1481        logger.info("applied weather preset %r", preset_id)
1482
1483    def apply_tuned_gains(self, job: str, params: dict) -> None:
1484        """Apply auto-tuned gains to the live controller (used by /api/tune)."""
1485        from .core.models import ControlModeName
1486
1487        c = self.controller
1488        if job == "heading":
1489            c.helm.pid.kp = float(params["heading_kp"])
1490            c.helm.pid.kd = float(params["heading_kd"])
1491            c.helm.pid.reset()
1492        elif job == "cruise":
1493            c.cruise_pid.kp = float(params["kp"])
1494            c.cruise_pid.ki = float(params["ki"])
1495            c.cruise_pid.reset()
1496        elif job == "drift":
1497            pid = c.modes[ControlModeName.DRIFT].pid
1498            pid.kp = float(params["kp"])
1499            pid.ki = float(params["ki"])
1500            pid.reset()
1501        elif job == "anchor":
1502            cfg = c.modes[ControlModeName.ANCHOR_HOLD].config
1503            cfg.kp = float(params["kp"])
1504            cfg.kd = float(params["kd"])
1505            cfg.idle_deadband_m = float(params["idle_deadband_m"])
1506        logger.info("applied tuned %s gains live: %s", job, params)
1507
1508    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1509    # Depth-map gridding (server-side averaging for the depth overlay)
1510    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1511    def depth_grid(self, cell_m: float = 15.0, bbox=None, field: str = "depth") -> dict:
1512        """Server-side gridded chart: bins soundings into ~``cell_m`` metre cells
1513        averaging the value per cell, so the UI can paint an averaged colour chart
1514        instead of 100k individual dots. ``cell_m`` is clamped to 2..200.
1515
1516        ``bbox`` = (west, south, east, north) limits the grid to that viewport
1517        window (Tier-1 windowing) so a large chart only ships what's on screen.
1518        ``field`` selects the layer: ``"depth"`` (default) or ``"hardness"``
1519        (bottom-hardness, raw 0..127) -- same gridding, different source.
1520
1521        Returns ``{ok, field, cell_m, min_depth, max_depth, count, cells}``; the
1522        chart changes slowly, so the UI polls this rather than the 5 Hz telemetry.
1523        """
1524        try:
1525            cell = float(cell_m)
1526        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1527            cell = 15.0
1528        cell = max(2.0, min(200.0, cell))
1529        source = self.depth_map.hardness if field == "hardness" else None
1530        grid = self.depth_map.as_grid(cell, bbox=bbox, source=source)
1531        grid["ok"] = True
1532        grid["field"] = field
1533        return grid
1534
1535    def depth_contours(self, bbox=None, limit: int = 20000) -> dict:
1536        """Imported depth contours (isobath polylines) windowed to a
1537        (west, south, east, north) bbox. Returns ``{ok, count, contours}`` where
1538        each contour is ``{d: depth_m, pts: [[lat, lon], ...]}``."""
1539        cs = self.depth_map.contours_in(bbox=bbox, limit=limit)
1540        return {"ok": True, "count": len(cs), "contours": cs}
1541
1542    def depth_composition(self, bbox=None, limit: int = 30000) -> dict:
1543        """Imported bottom-composition polygons, windowed to a
1544        (west, south, east, north) bbox. Returns ``{ok, count, polygons}`` where
1545        each is ``{pct: 0..100, ring: [[lat, lon], ...]}`` -- rendered FILLED
1546        (a vector polygon layer; not rasterised)."""
1547        ps = self.depth_map.composition_in(bbox=bbox, limit=limit)
1548        return {"ok": True, "count": len(ps), "polygons": ps}
1549
1550    def water_polygon(self, bbox) -> dict:
1551        """OSM water polygon(s) for a (west, south, east, north) bbox, used to
1552        CLIP the depth overlays to water (don't draw composition over land). Uses
1553        the same offline WaterCache as routing; fetches from Overpass + caches if
1554        absent (so offline it needs the area pre-downloaded). Returns
1555        ``{ok, water}`` where water is GeoJSON-style MultiPolygon coords
1556        ``[[[ [lon,lat], ... ]=exterior, [ ... ]=hole, ... ], ...]`` (empty if none)."""
1557        from .nav import water
1558        w, s, e, n = bbox
1559        wbbox = (s, w, n, e)                       # water.py order: (S, W, N, E)
1560        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1561        try:
1562            geom = cache.find_covering(wbbox)
1563            if geom is None:
1564                geom = water.assemble_water(water.fetch_overpass(*wbbox))
1565                if geom is not None and not geom.is_empty:
1566                    cache.store(wbbox, geom)
1567        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - network/parse; clip is optional
1568            logger.warning("water fetch for clip failed: %s", exc)
1569            return {"ok": False, "water": []}
1570        if geom is None or geom.is_empty:
1571            return {"ok": True, "water": []}
1572        # The cached water geometry can be 9-20 MB / ~930k vertices, but this is
1573        # a purely VISUAL clip mask, so coarse is fine. Shrink it before sending:
1574        #   (a) clip to a slightly-padded request bbox (cover a bit past the view)
1575        #   (b) simplify to a few-metre tolerance
1576        #   (c) round coords to 5 decimals (~1 m)
1577        import shapely.geometry as sgeom
1578
1579        pad = 0.10 * max(e - w, n - s)             # ~10% of the bbox span
1580        clip_box = sgeom.box(w - pad, s - pad, e + pad, n + pad)
1581        try:
1582            geom = geom.intersection(clip_box)
1583            geom = geom.simplify(1e-4, preserve_topology=True)  # ~11 m, coarse mask
1584        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - degenerate geom; clip is optional
1585            logger.warning("water clip/simplify failed: %s", exc)
1586            return {"ok": True, "water": []}
1587        if geom is None or geom.is_empty:
1588            return {"ok": True, "water": []}
1589        # Keep only polygonal parts (a clip can yield lines/points/collections).
1590        if geom.geom_type == "Polygon":
1591            polys = [geom]
1592        elif geom.geom_type == "MultiPolygon":
1593            polys = list(geom.geoms)
