1594        elif geom.geom_type == "GeometryCollection":
1595            polys = [g for g in geom.geoms if g.geom_type == "Polygon"]
1596        else:
1597            polys = []
1598        out = []
1599        for p in polys:
1600            if p.is_empty:
1601                continue
1602            rings = [list(p.exterior.coords)] + [list(r.coords) for r in p.interiors]
1603            out.append([[[round(x, 5), round(y, 5)] for (x, y) in ring] for ring in rings])
1604        return {"ok": True, "water": out}
1605
1606    def import_depth_map(self, filename: str, data: bytes, replace: bool = False) -> dict:
1607        """Import soundings from an uploaded open-format depth file (CSV/XYZ or
1608        GeoJSON). ``replace`` swaps the whole chart; otherwise the soundings are
1609        merged in. Persists to ``depthmap.json`` so the import survives restarts."""
1610        from .nav.depth import parse_depth_features
1611
1612        try:
1613            parsed = parse_depth_features(filename, data)
1614        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - any parse error -> clean message
1615            logger.warning("depth import parse failed: %s", exc)
1616            return {"ok": False, "error": f"could not parse the file: {exc}", "imported": 0}
1617        pts = parsed["soundings"]
1618        hard = parsed["hardness"]
1619        cont = parsed.get("contours", [])
1620        comp = parsed.get("composition", [])
1621        if not pts and not hard and not cont and not comp:
1622            return {"ok": False, "error": "no valid (lat, lon, depth) soundings found in the file",
1623                    "imported": 0}
1624        dm = self.depth_map
1625        if replace:
1626            dm.points = []
1627            dm.hardness = []
1628            dm.contours = []
1629            dm.composition = []
1630            dm._last = None
1631        dm.points.extend(pts)
1632        dm.hardness.extend(hard)
1633        dm.contours.extend(cont)
1634        dm.composition.extend(comp)
1635        if len(dm.points) > dm.max_points:
1636            dm.points = dm.points[-dm.max_points:]
1637        if len(dm.hardness) > dm.max_points:
1638            dm.hardness = dm.hardness[-dm.max_points:]
1639        dm.save(self._depth_map_path)           # soundings
1640        dm.save_chart(self._depth_chart_path)   # static chart (hardness/contours/composition)
1641        self._depth_saved_n = len(dm.points)
1642        logger.info("imported %d soundings + %d hardness + %d contours + %d composition from %s",
1643                    len(pts), len(hard), len(cont), len(comp), filename)
1644        return {"ok": True, "imported": len(pts), "hardness": len(hard),
1645                "contours": len(cont), "composition": len(comp), "total": len(dm.points)}
1646
1647    def telemetry(self) -> dict:
1648        # During replay, play recorded frames back instead of live state.
1649        if self.replay.active:
1650            frame = self.replay.current(time.time())
1651            if frame is not None:
1652                return frame
1653        # Auto-record the launch point on the first good fix (#61), then run the
1654        # periodic safety evaluations (battery RTL recommend + link failsafe).
1655        self.controller.maybe_record_launch()
1656        self.evaluate_rtl_recommend()
1657        self.evaluate_link_failsafe()
1658        # Trip log (#66): accumulate the current outing + run auto start/stop.
1659        self.trip.update(self.state.position, self.state.sog_knots, self._now_fn())
1660
1661        payload = self.state.to_dict()
1662        payload["safety"] = self.controller.safety_status.to_dict()
1663        payload["battery"] = self.battery_snapshot()
1664        payload["link"] = {
1665            "client_connected": self._ui_clients > 0,
1666            "since_s": (
1667                round(self._now_fn() - self._last_client_seen, 1)
1668                if self._last_client_seen is not None
1669                else None
1670            ),
1671            "failsafe_engaged": self._link_failsafe_engaged,
1672        }
1673        ctrl = self.controller
1674        payload["cruise"] = {
1675            "enabled": ctrl.cruise_knots is not None,
1676            "target_knots": ctrl.cruise_knots or 0.0,
1677        }
1678        payload["track"] = {
1679            "recording": ctrl.track.recording,
1680            "count": len(ctrl.track.points),
1681            # Most recent breadcrumbs for the map (cap the payload size).
1682            "points": [[p.lat, p.lon] for p in ctrl.track.points[-300:]],
1683        }
1684        payload["trip"] = self.trip.snapshot(self._now_fn())
1685        # Accumulate and expose the depth map; checkpoint to disk periodically
1686        # so soundings survive a crash/power loss on a real boat.
1687        # Record each sounding at the SAME position the boat marker is drawn at,
1688        # so the depth dots sit under the boat. In the sim the marker uses ground
1689        # truth -- and the sounder samples the bottom at that true position too --
1690        # whereas the GPS fix carries noise that would offset the dots beside the
1691        # boat. On real hardware there is no truth, so both use the GPS fix.
1692        sounding_pos = (
1693            self.simulator.truth().point
1694            if self.simulator is not None
1695            else self.state.position
1696        )
1697        self.depth_map.record(sounding_pos, self.state.depth_m)
1698        if len(self.depth_map.points) - self._depth_saved_n >= 25:
1699            self.depth_map.save(self._depth_map_path)
1700            self._depth_saved_n = len(self.depth_map.points)
1701        # depth_count is a cheap scalar; depth_points (~28 KB) is the bulk of the
1702        # frame. telemetry() returns the COMPLETE snapshot (so /api/state is
1703        # deterministic + full); the high-rate WS broadcaster decimates
1704        # depth_points to ~1 Hz (see ui/server.py:broadcaster). The frontend uses
1705        # depth_count for the readout and retains the last points when the WS
1706        # omits the array.
1707        payload["depth_count"] = len(self.depth_map.points)
1708        payload["depth_points"] = self.depth_map.as_list()
1709
1710        # Closed-loop steering unit: target (pre-slew) vs feedback (actual head
1711        # angle). On hardware the feedback is the AS5600; in sim it's the
1712        # slew-limited applied steering.
1713        boat = self.config.boat
1714        max_ang = self.state.max_steer_angle_deg
1715        actual = self.state.motor_command.steering
1716        target_deg = self.controller.safety.desired_steering * max_ang
1717        angle_deg = actual * max_ang
1718        rng = max(1.0, boat.steer_range_deg)
1719        payload["steering"] = {
1720            "commanded": round(actual, 3),
1721            "target_deg": round(target_deg, 1),
1722            "angle_deg": round(angle_deg, 1),
1723            "rate_dps": boat.max_steer_rate_dps,
1724            "range_deg": boat.steer_range_deg,
1725            "wrap_pct": round(max(-100.0, min(100.0, angle_deg / rng * 100.0)), 0),
1726            "feedback_ok": True,
1727        }
1728        # On real hardware the steering Arduino reports its *measured* azimuth
1729        # back over serial (#83). SerialMotorController parses those ``A`` lines
1730        # into ``last_feedback``; when present we surface the real feedback
1731        # instead of the commanded estimate. The sim motor has no such
1732        # attribute, so the simulator path keeps the modelled values above.
1733        feedback = getattr(self.controller.motor, "last_feedback", None)
1734        if feedback is not None:
1735            payload["steering"]["angle_deg"] = round(feedback.angle_deg, 1)
1736            payload["steering"]["wrap_pct"] = round(feedback.wrap_pct, 0)
1737            payload["steering"]["feedback_ok"] = feedback.ok
1738        payload["boat"] = self.boat_profile()
1739        payload["gps_offset"] = {
1740            "dlat": self.navigator.gps_dlat,
1741            "dlon": self.navigator.gps_dlon,
1742            "active": self.navigator.gps_offset_active,
1743        }
1744        payload["throttle_override"] = {
1745            "active": self.controller.throttle_override is not None,
1746            "percent": (
1747                self.controller.throttle_override * 100.0
1748                if self.controller.throttle_override is not None
1749                else 0.0
1750            ),
1751        }
1752        # Guided pattern modes (#57/#58/#59) -- expose each mode's live state.
1753        contour_mode = ctrl.modes[ControlModeName.CONTOUR_FOLLOW]
1754        payload["contour"] = {
1755            "target_depth_m": round(self.state.contour_target_depth_m, 1),
1756            "depth_m": round(self.state.depth_m, 1),
1757            "error_m": round(contour_mode.error_m, 2),
1758        }
1759        orbit_mode = ctrl.modes[ControlModeName.ORBIT]
1760        payload["orbit"] = {
1761            "center_lat": (
1762                self.state.orbit_center.lat if self.state.orbit_center else None
1763            ),
1764            "center_lon": (
1765                self.state.orbit_center.lon if self.state.orbit_center else None
1766            ),
1767            "radius_m": round(self.state.orbit_radius_m, 1),
1768            "direction": self.state.orbit_direction,
1769            "range_m": round(orbit_mode.range_m, 2),
1770        }
1771        trolling_mode = ctrl.modes[ControlModeName.TROLLING]
1772        payload["trolling"] = {
1773            "base_heading": round(self.state.trolling_base_heading, 2),
1774            "amplitude_deg": round(self.state.trolling_amplitude_deg, 1),
1775            "period_s": round(self.state.trolling_period_s, 1),
1776            "phase": round(trolling_mode.phase, 3),
1777        }
1778        payload["nav"] = {
1779            "paused": self.controller.suspended is not None,
1780            "suspended_mode": (
1781                self.controller.suspended["mode"].value
1782                if self.controller.suspended is not None
1783                else None
1784            ),
1785        }
1786        payload["calibration"] = self.calibration.snapshot()
1787        payload["sim_enabled"] = self.simulator is not None
1788        if self.simulator is not None:
1789            truth = self.simulator.truth()
1790            env = self.simulator.environment
1791            payload["truth"] = {
1792                "lat": truth.point.lat,
1793                "lon": truth.point.lon,
1794                "heading_deg": round(truth.heading_deg, 2),
1795                "speed_mps": round(truth.speed_mps, 3),
1796            }
1797            payload["environment"] = {
1798                "current_speed": env.current_speed,
1799                "current_dir": env.current_dir,
1800                "wind_speed": round(env.wind_speed, 2),
1801                "wind_dir": round(env.wind_dir, 1),
1802                "gust_amplitude_mps": env.gust_amplitude_mps,
1803                # Slow-wander amount so the UI can show how variable it is.
1804                "wind_variability": env.wind_variability,
1805                "current_variability": env.current_variability,
1806                # Instantaneous gusty wind the boat actually feels right now.
1807                "wind_gust_now": round(env.wind_speed + self.simulator.current_gust_mps, 2),
1808            }
1809        payload["debug"] = self.debug.status()
1810        payload["replay"] = {"active": self.replay.active, "name": self.replay.name} if self.replay.active else {"active": False}
1811        if self.debug.active:
1812            self.debug.write("telemetry", payload, time.time())
1813        return payload
1814
1815    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1816    # Lifecycle
1817    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1818    async def start(self) -> None:
1819        self.recorder.start()
1820        if self.nmea_tcp is not None:
1821            # A taken NMEA-TCP port must not crash the whole app on boot.
1822            try:
1823                await self.nmea_tcp.start()
1824                logger.info("NMEA TCP server listening on port %s", self.nmea_tcp.bound_port)
1825            except Exception:
1826                logger.exception("NMEA TCP server failed to start; continuing without it")
1827        # A serial device that won't open (e.g. an unplugged GPS or a renamed
1828        # port) must NOT take the whole app down on boot — log it and carry on so
1829        # the UI stays reachable to fix the device config (which reloads live).
1830        async def _try_start(name: str, dev) -> None:
1831            if dev is None:
1832                return
1833            try:
1834                await dev.start()
1835            except Exception:
1836                logger.exception("device %s failed to start; continuing without it", name)
1837        await _try_start("gps", self.gps)
1838        await _try_start("compass", self.compass)
1839        await _try_start("depth", self.depth_sounder)
1840        if self.simulator is not None:
1841            self._sim_task = asyncio.ensure_future(self.simulator.run())
1842            self._tasks.append(self._sim_task)
1843        self._tasks.append(asyncio.ensure_future(self.controller.run()))
1844        logger.info("runtime started (model=%s, hardware=%s)", self.config.sim.model, self.config.hardware.enabled)
1845
1846    async def stop(self) -> None:
1847        self.debug.stop()
1848        self.depth_map.save(self._depth_map_path)
1849        if self.simulator is not None:
1850            self.simulator.stop()
1851        self.controller.stop()
1852        if self.gps is not None:
1853            await self.gps.stop()
1854        if self.compass is not None:
1855            await self.compass.stop()
1856        if self.depth_sounder is not None:
1857            await self.depth_sounder.stop()
1858        if self.nmea_tcp is not None:
1859            await self.nmea_tcp.stop()
1860        for task in self._tasks:
1861            task.cancel()
1862        for task in self._tasks:
1863            with contextlib.suppress(asyncio.CancelledError):
1864                await task
1865        self.recorder.stop()
1866        logger.info("runtime stopped")
1867
1868
1869def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> None:
1870    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Vanchor-NG server")
1871    parser.add_argument("--config", default=None, help="YAML/JSON config file")
1872    parser.add_argument("--host", default=None)
1873    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=None)
1874    parser.add_argument("--model", default=None, choices=["simple", "fossen"])
1875    parser.add_argument("--hardware", action="store_true", help="use real serial devices")
1876    parser.add_argument("--nmea-tcp", action="store_true", help="accept NMEA over TCP")
1877    parser.add_argument("--log-level", default=None)
1878    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
1879
1880    config = load(args.config)
1881    # A saved device config (devices.json under data_dir) overrides the loaded
1882    # base hardware/nmea_tcp config before the runtime builds any device, so an
1883    # API-edited setup survives restarts. CLI flags below still win.
1884    apply_device_overrides(config)
1885    if args.host:
1886        config.server.host = args.host
1887    if args.port:
1888        config.server.port = args.port
1889    if args.model:
1890        config.sim.model = args.model
1891    if args.hardware:
1892        config.hardware.enabled = True
1893    if args.nmea_tcp:
1894        config.nmea_tcp.enabled = True
1895    if args.log_level:
1896        config.log_level = args.log_level
1897
1898    import uvicorn
1899
1900    from .ui.server import create_app
1901
1902    runtime = Runtime(config)
1903    app = create_app(runtime)
1904    uvicorn.run(
1905        app,
1906        host=config.server.host,
1907        port=config.server.port,
1908        log_level=(args.log_level or "info").lower(),
1909    )
1910
1911
1912if __name__ == "__main__":
1913    main()
logger = <Logger vanchor.app (WARNING)>
class Runtime: 
 187class Runtime:
 188    """Owns every component and the background tasks that drive them."""
 189
 190    def __init__(self, config: AppConfig | None = None, *, now_fn=time.time) -> None:
 191        self.config = config or AppConfig()
 192        cfg = self.config
 193
 194        # Injectable monotonic-ish clock so the link-loss failsafe (#64) can be
 195        # driven deterministically in tests instead of by wall-clock.
 196        self._now_fn = now_fn
 197
 198        observability.setup_logging(getattr(cfg, "log_level", "INFO"))
 199        self.bus = EventBus()
 200        observability.wiretap(self.bus)
 201        self.recorder = observability.TelemetryRecorder(ring_size=600)
 202
 203        # Debug session recorder + replay (records telemetry/nmea/commands/logs
 204        # to a gzipped NDJSON file for download + replay).
 205        from .core.debug_recorder import DebugRecorder, ReplayPlayer
 206
 207        self.debug = DebugRecorder(cfg.data_dir)
 208        self.replay = ReplayPlayer()
 209        self.bus.subscribe(events.NMEA_IN, self._record_nmea)
 210        logging.getLogger("vanchor").addHandler(_DebugLogHandler(self.debug))
 211
 212        self.state = NavigationState()
 213        self.state.anchor_radius_m = cfg.control.anchor_radius_m
 214        self.state.max_steer_angle_deg = cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg
 215        self.state.drift_target_knots = cfg.control.drift_default_knots
 216
 217        environment = Environment(
 218            current_speed=cfg.environment.current_speed,
 219            current_dir=cfg.environment.current_dir,
 220            wind_speed=cfg.environment.wind_speed,
 221            wind_dir=cfg.environment.wind_dir,
 222            gust_amplitude_mps=cfg.environment.gust_amplitude_mps,
 223            gust_tau_s=cfg.environment.gust_tau_s,
 224            wind_variability=cfg.environment.wind_variability,
 225            current_variability=cfg.environment.current_variability,
 226        )
 227
 228        # --- devices: simulated and/or real serial hardware (per-device) -- #
 229        # Built via _construct_devices so the SAME logic powers a live reload
 230        # (reload_devices) when the device config changes — no process restart.
 231        self.nmea_tcp = None
 232        self._environment = environment      # reused when devices are rebuilt live
 233        self._sim_task: "asyncio.Task | None" = None
 234        dev = self._construct_devices(cfg)
 235        self.simulator = dev["simulator"]
 236        self.gps = dev["gps"]
 237        self.compass = dev["compass"]
 238        self.depth_sounder = dev["depth_sounder"]
 239        motor = dev["motor"]
 240
 241        # Accumulates depth soundings for the auto depth-map overlay.
 242        self.depth_map = DepthMap()
 243        self._depth_map_path = os.path.join(cfg.data_dir, "depthmap.json")
 244        self._depth_chart_path = os.path.join(cfg.data_dir, "depthchart.json")
 245        self.depth_map.load(self._depth_map_path, self._depth_chart_path)
 246        self._depth_saved_n = len(self.depth_map.points)
 247
 248        # --- navigator + controller (identical for sim or hardware) ------- #
 249        self.navigator = Navigator(
 250            self.state,
 251            self.bus,
 252            SensorGuardConfig(
 253                position_jump_max_m=cfg.sensors.position_jump_max_m,
 254                heading_jump_max_deg=cfg.sensors.heading_jump_max_deg,
 255            ),
 256        )
 257        self.controller = Controller(
 258            self.state,
 259            motor,
 260            self.bus,
 261            tick_hz=cfg.control.tick_hz,
 262            helm=Helm(
 263                PID(
 264                    kp=cfg.control.heading_kp,
 265                    ki=cfg.control.heading_ki,
 266                    kd=cfg.control.heading_kd,
 267                    output_min=-1.0,
 268                    output_max=1.0,
 269                ),
 270                steer_tau=cfg.control.steer_tau,
 271                autopilot_steer_scale=(
 272                    cfg.boat.autopilot_steer_deg / cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg
 273                    if cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg > 0 else 1.0
 274                ),
 275                # Stern mounts yaw the opposite way -> flip the steering sign so
 276                # the autopilot turns the boat the right way.
 277                steer_sign=1.0 if cfg.boat.thruster_x_m() >= 0 else -1.0,
 278                # Pre-cancel the yaw a laterally-offset motor makes under thrust.
 279                thrust_yaw_ff=_thrust_yaw_ff_norm(cfg),
 280            ),
 281            anchor_config=AnchorConfig(
 282                kp=cfg.control.anchor_kp,
 283                kd=cfg.control.anchor_kd,
 284                idle_deadband_m=cfg.control.anchor_idle_deadband_m,
 285                boat_max_speed_mps=cfg.boat.max_speed_mps,
 286            ),
 287            waypoint_config=WaypointConfig(
 288                arrival_radius_m=cfg.control.waypoint_arrival_m,
 289                throttle=cfg.control.waypoint_throttle,
 290                xte_gain=cfg.control.waypoint_xte_gain,
 291            ),
 292            follow_apb_config=FollowApbConfig(throttle=cfg.control.waypoint_throttle),
 293            drift_config=DriftConfig(kp=cfg.control.drift_kp, ki=cfg.control.drift_ki),
 294            safety_config=SafetyConfig(
 295                max_thrust_slew_per_s=cfg.safety.max_thrust_slew_per_s,
 296                # Steering can't rotate faster than the head physically does.
 297                max_steer_slew_per_s=cfg.boat.max_steer_rate_dps / cfg.boat.max_steer_angle_deg,
 298                reverse_delay_s=cfg.safety.reverse_delay_s,
 299                fix_timeout_s=cfg.safety.fix_timeout_s,
 300                fix_failsafe_enabled=cfg.safety.fix_failsafe_enabled,
 301                drag_alarm_factor=cfg.safety.drag_alarm_factor,
 302                min_depth_m=cfg.safety.min_depth_m,
 303                nogo_lookahead_m=cfg.safety.nogo_lookahead_m,
 304            ),
 305            cruise_pid=PID(
 306                kp=cfg.control.cruise_kp,
 307                ki=cfg.control.cruise_ki,
 308                kd=0.0,
 309                output_min=0.0,
 310                output_max=1.0,
 311            ),
 312            jog_increment_m=cfg.control.jog_increment_m,
 313            track_min_distance_m=cfg.control.track_min_distance_m,
 314        )
 315
 316        if cfg.nmea_tcp.enabled:
 317            from .nav.nmea_net import NmeaTcpServer
 318
 319            self.nmea_tcp = NmeaTcpServer(
 320                self.bus, host=cfg.nmea_tcp.host, port=cfg.nmea_tcp.port
 321            )
 322
 323        # --- Trip log (#66): per-outing track + stats, persisted to disk. - #
 324        self.trip = TripLog(
 325            cfg.data_dir,
 326            min_distance_m=cfg.control.trip_min_distance_m,
 327            auto=cfg.control.auto_trip,
 328            start_speed_kn=cfg.control.trip_start_speed_kn,
 329            idle_timeout_s=cfg.control.trip_idle_timeout_s,
 330        )
 331
 332        self._tasks: list[asyncio.Task] = []
 333        self.calibration = CalibrationRunner(self)
 334
 335        # --- Named boat profiles (#75, #89): persisted, selectable spec bundles.
 336        # On first run (no boats.json) seed a small set of ready-to-pick presets
 337        # with the bow trolling motor active; never clobber a user's saved
 338        # profiles. Then apply whichever profile is marked active so a saved
 339        # selection survives a restart.
 340        from .core.boat_profiles import BoatProfileStore
 341
 342        self.boats = BoatProfileStore(cfg.data_dir)
 343        active = self.boats.active()
 344        if active is not None:
 345            self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 346
 347        # --- Lost-connection failsafe (#64) ------------------------------ #
 348        # Number of connected UI clients and the last time one was seen alive.
 349        self._ui_clients = 0
 350        self._last_client_seen: float | None = None
 351        # True once the failsafe has auto-engaged hold-position (so we don't
 352        # repeatedly re-engage it; cleared on reconnect).
 353        self._link_failsafe_engaged = False
 354        # Route-planning cancellation flag (#54): set by cancel_route_plan(),
 355        # reset at the start of every plan_route() call.
 356        self._route_plan_cancelled = False
 357
 358    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 359    # Boat profile (Init-boat wizard)
 360    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 361    def boat_profile(self) -> dict:
 362        b = self.config.boat
 363        return {
 364            "length_m": b.length_m,
 365            "beam_m": b.beam_m,
 366            "mass_kg": b.mass_kg,
 367            "max_speed_mps": b.max_speed_mps,
 368            "max_thrust_n": b.max_thrust_n,
 369            "thruster_mount": b.thruster_mount,
 370            "thruster_offset_m": b.thruster_offset_m,
 371            "thruster_y_m": b.thruster_y_m,
 372            "thrust_yaw_ff": b.thrust_yaw_ff,
 373            "thrust_yaw_ff_trim": b.thrust_yaw_ff_trim,
 374            "max_steer_angle_deg": b.max_steer_angle_deg,
 375            "max_turn_rate_deg": b.max_turn_rate_deg,
 376            "hull_tracking": b.hull_tracking,
 377            "shaft_dia_mm": b.shaft_dia_mm,
 378            "steer_range_deg": b.steer_range_deg,
 379            "steer_reduction": b.steer_reduction,
 380            "sonar_cone_deg": b.sonar_cone_deg,
 381            # The currently-active named profile (#75) so the UI can highlight it.
 382            "active_boat_id": getattr(self, "boats", None) and self.boats.active_id,
 383        }
 384
 385    def _apply_boat_specs(self, specs: dict) -> None:
 386        """Write a spec dict onto ``config.boat`` and make every live-applicable
 387        field take effect on the running sim/controller.
 388
 389        Numeric/string fields are coerced to ``BoatConfig``'s declared types.
 390        After writing the config we *rebuild* the simulator's physics params via
 391        :func:`_build_boat_params` (not just poke ``max_speed_mps``) so changing
 392        mass, thrust, geometry etc. actually changes the boat's behaviour -- the
 393        Fossen model derives its damping + mass matrices from these at build
 394        time, so an in-place tweak alone would be ignored.
 395        """
 396        b = self.config.boat
 397        for key, val in specs.items():
 398            if hasattr(b, key) and val is not None:
 399                cur = getattr(b, key)
 400                setattr(b, key, type(cur)(val) if isinstance(cur, (int, float)) else val)
 401
 402        # Steering authority / slew limits + hull-character control tuning.
 403        self.state.max_steer_angle_deg = b.max_steer_angle_deg
 404        # The hull character (directional stability) biases the AUTOPILOT TUNING,
 405        # so a boat starts sensibly tuned even on real hardware (where it can't
 406        # change the physics): a stiff, tracking hull (high hull_tracking) resists
 407        # turning -> use MORE steering authority and less command smoothing; a
 408        # loose, skittish hull -> LESS authority and more smoothing to avoid
 409        # hunting. This is a PRIOR -- the auto-calibration drive then measures the
 410        # real boat and refines from here. At hull_tracking == 1.0 it is a no-op.
 411        ht = min(3.0, max(0.25, b.hull_tracking))
 412        if b.max_steer_angle_deg > 0:
 413            self.controller.safety.config.max_steer_slew_per_s = (
 414                b.max_steer_rate_dps / b.max_steer_angle_deg
 415            )
 416            auth_deg = min(b.autopilot_steer_deg * ht, b.max_steer_angle_deg)
 417            self.controller.helm.autopilot_steer_scale = auth_deg / b.max_steer_angle_deg
 418        self.controller.helm.steer_tau = (
 419            self.config.control.steer_tau * min(1.8, max(0.6, ht ** -0.5))
 420        )
 421        # A bow vs stern mount flips which way a steering deflection turns the
 422        # boat -- keep the helm's sign in step so switching profiles never leaves
 423        # the autopilot steering backwards.
 424        self.controller.helm.steer_sign = 1.0 if b.thruster_x_m() >= 0 else -1.0
 425        # Lateral-offset thrust-yaw feed-forward follows the geometry/trim live so
 426        # changing the offset (or the calibrated trim) updates compensation now.
 427        self.controller.helm.thrust_yaw_ff = _thrust_yaw_ff_norm(self.config)
 428        # Anchor mode caps thrust by the boat's top speed; keep it in step.
 429        anchor = self.controller.modes.get(ControlModeName.ANCHOR_HOLD)
 430        if anchor is not None and hasattr(anchor, "config"):
 431            anchor.config.boat_max_speed_mps = b.max_speed_mps
 432        # Waypoint mode's forward/reverse decision needs the boat's measured
 433        # speed, reverse efficiency, and turn rate.
 434        wp = self.controller.modes.get(ControlModeName.WAYPOINT)
 435        if wp is not None and hasattr(wp, "config"):
 436            wp.config.reverse_efficiency = b.reverse_efficiency
 437            wp.config.turn_rate_dps = b.max_turn_rate_deg
 438            wp.config.boat_speed_mps = b.max_speed_mps
 439
 440        # Rebuild the live physics params so mass/thrust/geometry changes bite.
 441        self._rebuild_boat_physics()
 442
 443    def _rebuild_boat_physics(self) -> None:
 444        """Swap the simulator boat's physics params for freshly-built ones.
 445
 446        The Fossen model precomputes its mass + damping matrices (and the
 447        derived surge drag / yaw inertia) in ``__post_init__``/``_build_matrices``
 448        from the params, so we replace ``params`` wholesale and re-derive rather
 449        than mutating fields in place. The simple kinematic model has no derived
 450        state, so swapping the dataclass is enough."""
 451        if self.simulator is None:
 452            return
 453        boat = self.simulator.boat
 454        params = _build_boat_params(self.config)
 455        boat.params = params
 456        # Re-derive the Fossen matrices for the new params (no-op for "simple").
 457        rebuild = getattr(boat, "_build_matrices", None)
 458        if callable(rebuild):
 459            rebuild()
 460
 461    def update_boat(self, fields: dict) -> dict:
 462        """Update the boat profile and apply what can change live.
 463
 464        Also persists the change back into the active named profile (#75) so the
 465        existing ``POST /api/boat`` path and the profile store stay in sync."""
 466        self._apply_boat_specs(fields)
 467        # Write the edited specs back into the active profile so they persist.
 468        if getattr(self, "boats", None) is not None:
 469            from .core.boat_profiles import specs_from_boat
 470
 471            self.boats.save(self.boats.active_id, None, specs_from_boat(self.config.boat))
 472        logger.info("boat profile updated: %s", fields)
 473        return self.boat_profile()
 474
 475    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 476    # Named boat profiles (#75)
 477    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 478    def boat_profiles_list(self) -> dict:
 479        """``{active_id, profiles:[{id,name,...specs}, ...]}``."""
 480        return self.boats.to_dict()
 481
 482    def boat_profiles_create(self, name: str, specs: dict | None = None) -> dict:
 483        """Create a profile (specs default to the current active boat). Returns
 484        ``{id, ...}`` of the new profile."""
 485        from .core.boat_profiles import specs_from_boat
 486
 487        if specs is None:
 488            specs = specs_from_boat(self.config.boat)
 489        pid = self.boats.create(name, specs)
 490        return self.boats.get(pid) or {"id": pid}
 491
 492    def boat_profiles_update(
 493        self, profile_id: str, name: str | None = None, specs: dict | None = None
 494    ) -> dict | None:
 495        """Update a profile's name/specs. If the edited profile is the active
 496        one, also apply the new specs live. Returns the updated profile or None
 497        if the id is unknown."""
 498        if not self.boats.save(profile_id, name, specs):
 499            return None
 500        if profile_id == self.boats.active_id:
 501            active = self.boats.active()
 502            if active is not None:
 503                self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 504        return self.boats.get(profile_id)
 505
 506    def boat_profiles_activate(self, profile_id: str) -> dict | None:
 507        """Make a profile active and apply its specs to the live sim. Returns
 508        the applied boat profile dict, or None if the id is unknown."""
 509        if not self.boats.set_active(profile_id):
 510            return None
 511        active = self.boats.active()
 512        if active is not None:
 513            self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 514        logger.info("activated boat profile %s", profile_id)
 515        return self.boat_profile()
 516
 517    def boat_profiles_delete(self, profile_id: str) -> bool:
 518        """Delete a profile (refuses the last one). If the deleted profile was
 519        active, apply whatever profile is active afterwards."""
 520        if not self.boats.delete(profile_id):
 521            return False
 522        active = self.boats.active()
 523        if active is not None:
 524            self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 525        return True
 526
 527    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 528    # Versioned backup / restore of all persistent state
 529    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 530    def create_backup(self, client: dict | None = None, *, created_at: str | None = None) -> bytes:
 531        """Build a versioned backup ZIP of this runtime's ``data_dir`` (boats,
 532        depth map, devices, trips) plus the UI's ``client`` localStorage slice.
 533
 534        ``created_at`` is an ISO8601 string the caller supplies (the endpoint
 535        passes the request time); when omitted we use the injected clock to make
 536        a UTC timestamp -- the backup module itself never calls ``datetime.now``.
 537        Returns the raw ``.zip`` bytes."""
 538        from .core import backup
 539
 540        if created_at is None:
 541            created_at = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime(self._now_fn()))
 542        return backup.create_backup(
 543            self.config.data_dir, client=client, created_at=created_at
 544        )
 545
 546    def restore_backup(self, zip_bytes: bytes) -> dict:
 547        """Restore a backup ZIP into ``data_dir`` and reload what it can LIVE.
 548
 549        Extracts the archive (overwriting the on-disk files), then refreshes the
 550        in-memory state it can without a restart: re-loads the boat profiles +
 551        the depth map from disk and re-applies the active profile, and reloads
 552        the device config. Anything that can't be refreshed live sets
 553        ``restart_required``. Returns the backup-module result dict plus
 554        ``restart_required``. Raises :class:`ValueError` (-> 400) on a bad zip."""
 555        from .core import backup
 556
 557        result = backup.restore_backup(self.config.data_dir, zip_bytes)
 558        restart_required = False
 559
 560        # Boat profiles: rebuild the store from the restored boats.json and
 561        # re-apply the active profile so the live physics follow it.
 562        try:
 563            from .core.boat_profiles import BoatProfileStore
 564
 565            self.boats = BoatProfileStore(self.config.data_dir)
 566            active = self.boats.active()
 567            if active is not None:
 568                self._apply_boat_specs(active["specs"])
 569        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
 570            logger.exception("restore: reloading boat profiles failed")
 571            restart_required = True
 572
 573        # Depth map: reload the restored soundings from disk.
 574        try:
 575            self.depth_map = DepthMap()
 576            self.depth_map.load(self._depth_map_path, self._depth_chart_path)
 577            self._depth_saved_n = len(self.depth_map.points)
 578        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
 579            logger.exception("restore: reloading depth map failed")
 580            restart_required = True
 581
 582        # Device config: re-read the restored devices.json into the live config
 583        # and rebuild the device set (no restart). reload_devices is async, so
 584        # schedule it; if there's no running loop, defer to a restart.
 585        apply_device_overrides(self.config)
 586        try:
 587            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
 588            loop.create_task(self.reload_devices())
 589        except RuntimeError:
 590            # No event loop (e.g. a synchronous restore in a test) -> the new
 591            # device config will take effect on the next start/restart.
 592            restart_required = True
 593
 594        result["restart_required"] = restart_required
 595        logger.info("backup restored (restart_required=%s)", restart_required)
 596        return result
 597
 598    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 599    # Device / hardware config (persisted, editable over the API)
 600    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 601    # The valid values for each source field (used to validate edits + to tell
 602    # the UI which options to offer). Sensors share one set; the motor adds
 603    # "both" (drive the sim boat AND mirror to a real servo).
 604    _SENSOR_SOURCES = ("sim", "serial", "nmea")
 605    _MOTOR_SOURCES = ("sim", "serial", "both")
 606
 607    def _compass_sources(self) -> tuple:
 608        """Built-in compass sources + any registered driver sources (e.g.
 609        ``hwt901b``). Registered drivers are discovered from the plugin registry,
 610        so a new compass driver adds itself here without editing this file."""
 611        from .hardware import registry
 612        return self._SENSOR_SOURCES + tuple(registry.sources("compass"))
 613
 614    def device_config(self) -> dict:
 615        """Current device/hardware config + the selectable options.
 616
 617        Shape matches what :meth:`set_device_config` persists, plus ``options``
 618        (for the UI's selects) and ``restart_required`` (always ``False`` on a
 619        plain read; a POST returns ``True`` because devices are rebuilt only on
 620        restart, not hot-swapped)."""
 621        return {
 622            "hardware": asdict(self.config.hardware),
 623            "nmea_tcp": asdict(self.config.nmea_tcp),
 624            "options": {
 625                "sensor": list(self._SENSOR_SOURCES),
 626                "compass": list(self._compass_sources()),
 627                "motor": list(self._MOTOR_SOURCES),
 628            },
 629            "menus": self._device_menus(),
 630            "driver_menus": self._driver_menus(),
 631            "restart_required": False,
 632        }
 633
 634    def set_device_config(self, payload: dict) -> dict:
 635        """Validate, persist, and apply a device-config edit.
 636
 637        ``payload`` is ``{"hardware": {...}, "nmea_tcp": {...}}`` (either key
 638        optional). Validates source values + field types, writes
 639        ``devices.json``, and updates the in-memory ``config.hardware`` /
 640        ``config.nmea_tcp`` so a subsequent read reflects it. Devices are NOT
 641        hot-swapped; the change applies on the next restart, so the returned
 642        ``restart_required`` is ``True``. Raises :class:`ValueError` on a bad
 643        payload (the endpoint maps it to a 400)."""
 644        if not isinstance(payload, dict):
 645            raise ValueError("payload must be an object")
 646        hw_in = payload.get("hardware") or {}
 647        nmea_in = payload.get("nmea_tcp") or {}
 648        if not isinstance(hw_in, dict) or not isinstance(nmea_in, dict):
 649            raise ValueError("'hardware' and 'nmea_tcp' must be objects")
 650
 651        # Build validated copies off the *current* config (so an edit can be
 652        # partial). Sources: sensors sim|serial|nmea, motor sim|serial|both.
 653        hw = HardwareConfig(**asdict(self.config.hardware))
 654        for dev in ("gps", "compass", "depth"):
 655            key = f"{dev}_source"
 656            allowed = self._compass_sources() if dev == "compass" else self._SENSOR_SOURCES
 657            if hw_in.get(key) is not None and hw_in[key] not in allowed:
 658                raise ValueError(
 659                    f"{key} must be one of {allowed} (got {hw_in[key]!r})"
 660                )
 661        if hw_in.get("motor_source") is not None and hw_in["motor_source"] not in self._MOTOR_SOURCES:
 662            raise ValueError(
 663                f"motor_source must be one of {self._MOTOR_SOURCES} (got {hw_in['motor_source']!r})"
 664            )
 665        # Ports are strings; baudrate is an int. Coerce/validate via the merge.
 666        for key in ("gps_port", "compass_port", "motor_port"):
 667            if key in hw_in and hw_in[key] is not None and not isinstance(hw_in[key], str):
 668                raise ValueError(f"{key} must be a string")
 669        for key, src in (("baudrate", hw_in), ("port", nmea_in)):
 670            if key in src and src[key] is not None:
 671                try:
 672                    int(src[key])
 673                except (TypeError, ValueError):
 674                    raise ValueError(f"{key} must be an integer") from None
 675
 676        nmea = NmeaTcpConfig(**asdict(self.config.nmea_tcp))
 677        _merge_into(hw, hw_in)
 678        _merge_into(nmea, nmea_in)
 679
 680        save_device_overrides(self.config.data_dir, hw, nmea)
 681        # Reflect the edit in the live config so a subsequent GET shows it
 682        # (the actual devices are rebuilt only on restart).
 683        self.config.hardware = hw
 684        self.config.nmea_tcp = nmea
 685        logger.info("device config updated (restart required to apply): %s", payload)
 686        # Persisted + reflected in-memory; devices are rebuilt on the next start.
 687        return {"ok": True, "restart_required": True}
 688
 689    def _build_serial_gps(self, cfg: AppConfig):
 690        from .hardware.serial_devices import SerialGps
 691        from .hardware.serial_link import PySerialTransport
 692        hw = cfg.hardware
 693        return SerialGps(PySerialTransport(hw.gps_port, baudrate=hw.baudrate), self.bus)
 694
 695    def _device_menus(self) -> list:
 696        """Collect device-specific menus (settings/actions) from the active
 697        devices that expose ``device_menu()`` -- surfaced to the UI so a driver
 698        can offer its own controls (e.g. the HWT901B compass)."""
 699        out: list = []
 700        for dev in (self.gps, self.compass, self.depth_sounder):
 701            fn = getattr(dev, "device_menu", None)
 702            if callable(fn):
 703                try:
 704                    out.append(fn())
 705                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - a bad menu can't break config
 706                    logger.warning("device_menu failed: %s", exc)
 707        return out
 708
 709    def _driver_menus(self) -> dict:
 710        """Per-source device-menu SCHEMAS from registered drivers, with any saved
 711        settings overlaid -- so the UI can render a device's menu the moment its
 712        source is selected, before any instance exists. Keyed by source name."""
 713        out: dict = {}
 714        saved_all = self.config.hardware.device_settings or {}
 715        for kind in ("compass",):  # device kinds with pluggable driver menus
 716            for src, schema in registry.menus(kind).items():
 717                out[src] = _overlay_menu_values(schema, saved_all.get(kind, {}))
 718        return out
 719
 720    def _device_by_kind(self, kind: str):
 721        return {"gps": self.gps, "compass": self.compass,
 722                "depth": self.depth_sounder}.get(kind)
 723
 724    def apply_device_setting(self, kind: str, key: str, value) -> dict:
 725        """Persist a device-menu setting for ``kind`` and apply it live if the
 726        device is running. Persisted settings are read when the device is
 727        (re)built, so a choice sticks even when the device isn't active yet."""
 728        fn = getattr(self._device_by_kind(kind), "apply_setting", None)
 729        known = any(
 730            key in {s.get("key") for s in menu.get("settings", [])}
 731            for menu in registry.menus(kind).values()
 732        )
 733        if not known and not callable(fn):
 734            return {"ok": False, "message": f"no settings for device {kind!r}"}
 735        ds = self.config.hardware.device_settings
 736        ds.setdefault(kind, {})[key] = value
 737        try:
 738            save_device_overrides(self.config.data_dir, self.config.hardware,
 739                                  self.config.nmea_tcp)
 740        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 741            return {"ok": False, "message": f"could not save: {exc}"}
 742        # Apply to the running device too, if there is one that accepts it.
 743        live = fn(key, value) if callable(fn) else None
 744        applied_live = bool(live and live.get("ok"))
 745        return {"ok": True, "saved": True, "applied_live": applied_live,
 746                "restart_required": not applied_live}
 747
 748    def run_device_action(self, kind: str, name: str, params: dict | None = None) -> dict:
 749        """Run a device-menu action on the active device of ``kind``."""
 750        fn = getattr(self._device_by_kind(kind), "run_action", None)
 751        if not callable(fn):
 752            return {"ok": False, "message": f"no actions for device {kind!r}"}
 753        try:
 754            return fn(name, params or {})
 755        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 756            return {"ok": False, "message": str(exc)}
 757
 758    def _build_serial_compass(self, cfg: AppConfig):
 759        from .hardware.serial_devices import SerialCompass
 760        from .hardware.serial_link import PySerialTransport
 761        hw = cfg.hardware
 762        return SerialCompass(PySerialTransport(hw.compass_port, baudrate=hw.baudrate), self.bus)
 763
 764    def _build_serial_motor(self, cfg: AppConfig):
 765        from .hardware.serial_devices import SerialMotorController
 766        from .hardware.serial_link import PySerialTransport
 767        hw = cfg.hardware
 768        return SerialMotorController(PySerialTransport(hw.motor_port, baudrate=hw.baudrate))
 769
 770    def _construct_devices(self, cfg: AppConfig) -> dict:
 771        """Build the device set (simulator + sensors + motor) for ``cfg.hardware``.
 772        Returns a dict and does NOT mutate ``self`` — so a live reload can build
 773        the new set first and swap it in only on success (see reload_devices)."""
 774        src = {n: cfg.hardware.source(n) for n in ("gps", "compass", "depth", "motor")}
 775        # The sim boat exists whenever any device is simulated (sensors read its
 776        # truth; the sim motor drives it).
 777        simulator = None
 778        if any(s in ("sim", "both") for s in src.values()):
 779            # On a LIVE reload, carry over the current boat state so a device
 780            # change doesn't teleport the simulated boat back to the start.
 781            prev = getattr(self, "simulator", None)
 782            start_state = (
 783                prev.truth() if prev is not None
 784                else BoatState(point=GeoPoint(cfg.sim.start_lat, cfg.sim.start_lon), heading_deg=0.0)
 785            )
 786            simulator = Simulator(
 787                start=start_state,
 788                params=_build_boat_params(cfg),
 789                environment=self._environment,
 790                physics_hz=cfg.sim.physics_hz,
 791                time_scale=cfg.sim.time_scale,
 792                model=cfg.sim.model,
 793                battery_config=_build_battery_config(cfg),
 794            )
 795        sim_motor = simulator.motor if simulator is not None else None
 796        if src["motor"] == "serial":
 797            motor = self._build_serial_motor(cfg)
 798        elif src["motor"] == "both":
 799            motor = _TeeMotor([sim_motor, self._build_serial_motor(cfg)])
 800        else:
 801            motor = sim_motor
 802        # "nmea" (or anything not sim/serial) builds NO internal sensor: the
 803        # navigator is fed by external NMEA over the bridge/inject instead.
 804        gps = compass = depth = None
 805        if src["gps"] == "serial":
 806            gps = self._build_serial_gps(cfg)
 807        elif src["gps"] == "sim":
 808            gps = SimGps(simulator.truth, self.bus, update_hz=cfg.sensors.gps_hz,
 809                         position_noise_m=cfg.sensors.gps_noise_m)
 810        if src["compass"] == "serial":
 811            compass = self._build_serial_compass(cfg)
 812        elif src["compass"] == "sim":
 813            compass = SimCompass(simulator.truth, self.bus, update_hz=cfg.sensors.compass_hz,
 814                                 heading_noise_deg=cfg.sensors.compass_noise_deg)
 815        elif registry.has("compass", src["compass"]):
 816            # A pluggable driver builds eagerly (may open a port / import an
 817            # optional lib), so a failure here must NOT crash startup -- skip it,
 818            # log why, and leave the UI reachable to fix the config (mirrors the
 819            # serial "unopenable device" resilience). The warning shows in
 820            # Settings -> View logs.
 821            try:
 822                compass = registry.build_device("compass", src["compass"], self, cfg)
 823            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
 824                logger.warning(
 825                    "compass source %r could not be built (%s); running without a "
 826                    "compass. Change it in Settings -> Devices.", src["compass"], exc)
 827                compass = None
 828        if src["depth"] == "sim":
 829            depth = SimDepthSounder(
 830                simulator.truth,
 831                Bathymetry(origin=GeoPoint(cfg.sim.start_lat, cfg.sim.start_lon)),
 832                self.bus, update_hz=cfg.sensors.depth_hz)
 833        return {"simulator": simulator, "gps": gps, "compass": compass,
 834                "depth_sounder": depth, "motor": motor}
 835
 836    async def reload_devices(self) -> dict:
 837        """Rebuild the device set LIVE (no process restart) so a device-config
 838        change applies immediately. Builds + starts the NEW set first, and only
 839        stops the old + swaps in if that succeeds — so a bad serial port leaves
 840        the current devices running and the autopilot uninterrupted. Returns
 841        ``{applied: bool, error?: str}``."""
 842        try:
 843            new = self._construct_devices(self.config)
 844        except Exception as exc:  # e.g. a serial port that doesn't exist
 845            logger.exception("device reload: build failed")
 846            return {"applied": False, "error": str(exc)}
 847        started: list = []
 848        try:
 849            for d in (new["gps"], new["compass"], new["depth_sounder"]):
 850                if d is not None:
 851                    await d.start()
 852                    started.append(d)
 853            new_sim_task = (asyncio.ensure_future(new["simulator"].run())
 854                            if new["simulator"] is not None else None)
 855        except Exception as exc:
 856            logger.exception("device reload: start failed; rolling back")
 857            for d in started:
 858                with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
 859                    await d.stop()
 860            return {"applied": False, "error": str(exc)}
 861        # New set is live — now retire the old one and swap references.
 862        for d in (self.gps, self.compass, self.depth_sounder):
 863            if d is not None:
 864                with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
 865                    await d.stop()
 866        if self.simulator is not None:
 867            self.simulator.stop()
 868        if self._sim_task is not None:
 869            self._sim_task.cancel()
 870        self.gps, self.compass, self.depth_sounder = new["gps"], new["compass"], new["depth_sounder"]
 871        self.simulator, self._sim_task = new["simulator"], new_sim_task
 872        self.controller.motor = new["motor"]
 873        # Re-prime the navigator with a fresh fix/heading so the fix-lost failsafe
 874        # doesn't latch (and stop the motor) over the brief gap during the swap.
 875        for dev in (self.gps, self.compass):
 876            if dev is not None:
 877                with contextlib.suppress(Exception):
 878                    self.navigator.handle_sentence(dev.sample())
 879        logger.info("device config applied live")
 880        return {"applied": True}
 881
 882    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 883    # Commands (from the UI).
 884    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 885    async def _record_nmea(self, sentence: str) -> None:
 886        if self.debug.active:
 887            self.debug.write("nmea", sentence, time.time())
 888
 889    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 890    # Debug session recording + replay
 891    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 892    def start_debug(self, name: str | None = None) -> dict:
 893        nm = name or "session-" + time.strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S", time.localtime(time.time()))
 894        return self.debug.start(nm, time.time())
 895
 896    def stop_debug(self) -> dict:
 897        return self.debug.stop()
 898
 899    def start_replay(self, file_name: str) -> bool:
 900        path = self.debug.path_for(file_name)
 901        if path is None:
 902            return False
 903        return self.replay.load(path, time.time())
 904
 905    def stop_replay(self) -> None:
 906        self.replay.stop()
 907
 908    def handle_command(self, command: dict) -> None:
 909        ctype = command.get("type")
 910        if self.debug.active and ctype not in (None,):
 911            self.debug.write("command", command, time.time())
 912        if ctype == "set_environment" and self.simulator is not None:
 913            env = self.simulator.environment
 914            for key in (
 915                "current_speed",
 916                "current_dir",
 917                "wind_speed",
 918                "wind_dir",
 919                "gust_amplitude_mps",
 920                "wind_variability",
 921                "current_variability",
 922            ):
 923                if key in command:
 924                    setattr(env, key, float(command[key]))
 925            # Re-anchor the slow weather wander on the new base values.
 926            self.simulator.set_weather_base()
 927        elif ctype == "weather_preset" and self.simulator is not None:
 928            self._apply_weather_preset(str(command.get("id", "")))
 929        elif ctype == "teleport":
 930            self._teleport(command)
 931        elif ctype == "inject_nmea":
 932            asyncio.ensure_future(self.bus.publish(events.NMEA_IN, str(command["sentence"])))
 933        elif ctype == "set_gps_offset":
 934            self.navigator.set_gps_offset(
 935                float(command["true_lat"]), float(command["true_lon"])
 936            )
 937        elif ctype == "clear_gps_offset":
 938            self.navigator.clear_gps_offset()
 939        elif ctype == "load_route":
 940            self._load_route(command)
 941        elif ctype == "set_battery":
 942            self._set_battery(command.get("soc_pct"))
 943        elif ctype == "return_to_launch":
 944            self.return_to_launch()
 945        elif ctype == "trip_start":
 946            self.trip_start(command.get("name"))
 947        elif ctype == "trip_stop":
 948            self.trip_stop()
 949        else:
 950            self.controller.handle_command(command)
 951
 952    def _teleport(self, command: dict) -> None:
 953        """Sim teleport (#90): instantly snap the simulated boat's ground truth to
 954        a new ``lat``/``lon`` (and optional ``heading``), zeroing its velocity so
 955        it doesn't keep coasting. A safe no-op on real hardware (no simulator)."""
 956        if self.simulator is None:
 957            logger.info("teleport ignored (no simulator)")
 958            return
 959        heading = command.get("heading")
 960        self.simulator.teleport(
 961            float(command["lat"]),
 962            float(command["lon"]),
 963            float(heading) if heading is not None else None,
 964        )
 965        # Re-prime the GPS spike-guard so the next fix at the new spot snaps
 966        # straight through instead of being rejected as a position jump.
 967        guard = self.navigator.guard
 968        guard._last_point = None
 969        guard._pending_point = None
 970
 971    def _set_battery(self, soc_pct: object) -> None:
 972        """Set/reset the battery state-of-charge (#60). Sim-only: on real
 973        hardware the SOC comes from a battery monitor over the HAL."""
 974        if soc_pct is None or self.simulator is None:
 975            logger.info("set_battery ignored (no value or no sim battery)")
 976            return
 977        self.simulator.battery.set_soc(float(soc_pct))
 978        logger.info("battery SOC set to %.0f%%", float(soc_pct))
 979
 980    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 981    # Return-to-Launch (#61)
 982    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
 983    def return_to_launch(self) -> dict:
 984        """Plan a water route from the boat to its launch point and follow it,
 985        anchoring on arrival.
 986
 987        Returns the plan result dict. Synchronous + CPU/IO-heavy (water fetch +
 988        routing); call it from an executor on the live path.
 989        """
 990        launch = self.state.launch
 991        if launch is None:
 992            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": "No launch point set."}
 993        result = self.plan_route(launch.lat, launch.lon, mode="fastest")
 994        if not result.get("ok") or not result.get("waypoints"):
 995            return result
 996        self.state.waypoints = [
 997            Waypoint(name=str(w.get("name", "WP")), point=GeoPoint(w["lat"], w["lon"]))
 998            for w in result["waypoints"]
 999        ]
1000        self.controller.handle_command({"type": "load_route", "on_arrival": "anchor"})
1001        # load_route doesn't take on_arrival; set it explicitly on the state.
1002        self.state.route_on_arrival = "anchor"
1003        logger.info("Return-to-Launch engaged: %d waypoints home", len(self.state.waypoints))
1004        return result
1005
1006    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1007    # Trip log (#66)
1008    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1009    def trip_start(self, name: str | None = None) -> dict:
1010        """Manually start a trip (overrides/replaces any active one)."""
1011        trip = self.trip.start(name, self._now_fn())
1012        return self.trip.snapshot(self._now_fn())
1013
1014    def trip_stop(self) -> dict:
1015        """Manually stop + persist the active trip. No-op when none is active."""
1016        self.trip.stop(self._now_fn())
1017        return self.trip.snapshot(self._now_fn())
1018
1019    def trip_list(self) -> list[dict]:
1020        return self.trip.list_trips()
1021
1022    def trip_get(self, trip_id: str) -> dict | None:
1023        return self.trip.get_trip(trip_id)
1024
1025    def trip_gpx(self, trip_id: str) -> str | None:
1026        return self.trip.gpx(trip_id)
1027
1028    def trip_delete(self, trip_id: str) -> bool:
1029        return self.trip.delete_trip(trip_id)
1030
1031    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1032    # Battery (#60)
1033    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1034    def battery_snapshot(self) -> dict:
1035        """Battery telemetry. From the sim battery, or zeros if none (hardware
1036        battery monitor over the HAL will populate this later)."""
1037        if self.simulator is not None:
1038            return self.simulator.battery.to_dict()
1039        return {
1040            "soc_pct": 0.0,
1041            "voltage_v": 0.0,
1042            "current_a": 0.0,
1043            "draw_w": 0.0,
1044            "range_m": 0.0,
1045            "time_to_empty_s": None,
1046        }
1047
1048    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1049    # Lost-connection failsafe (#64) + RTL auto-recommend (#61)
1050    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1051    def client_connected(self) -> None:
1052        """A UI client connected; clear any link failsafe."""
1053        self._ui_clients += 1
1054        self._last_client_seen = self._now_fn()
1055        if self._link_failsafe_engaged:
1056            logger.info("UI client reconnected; link failsafe cleared")
1057        self._link_failsafe_engaged = False
1058
1059    def client_activity(self) -> None:
1060        """Mark the link alive (any inbound client traffic)."""
1061        self._last_client_seen = self._now_fn()
1062
1063    def client_disconnected(self) -> None:
1064        """A UI client disconnected."""
1065        self._ui_clients = max(0, self._ui_clients - 1)
1066        self._last_client_seen = self._now_fn()
1067
1068    def _underway(self) -> bool:
1069        """True when the boat is in a guided/cruising mode actively making way --
1070        i.e. not already idle-manual or holding station on anchor."""
1071        return self.state.mode in _UNDERWAY_MODES
1072
1073    def evaluate_link_failsafe(self, now: float | None = None) -> bool:
1074        """Engage hold-position if no UI client has been seen for the timeout
1075        while underway. Returns True if it engaged on this call. Idempotent and
1076        clock-injectable (pass ``now`` in tests)."""
1077        if now is None:
1078            now = self._now_fn()
1079        timeout = self.config.safety.link_loss_timeout_s
1080        connected = self._ui_clients > 0
1081        if connected or self._last_client_seen is None or self._link_failsafe_engaged:
1082            return False
1083        if not self._underway():
1084            return False
1085        if now - self._last_client_seen < timeout:
1086            return False
1087        # Lost the link while underway -> hold position (anchor-hold here).
1088        logger.warning("link lost %.0fs while underway; engaging hold-position", timeout)
1089        self.controller.handle_command({"type": "anchor_hold"})
1090        self._link_failsafe_engaged = True
1091        return True
1092
1093    def evaluate_rtl_recommend(self) -> bool:
1094        """Set ``state.rtl_recommended`` when the battery range has dropped to
1095        within ``rtl_margin_m`` of the distance home (so the boat can *just* make
1096        it back). If ``auto_rtl`` is set, engage RTL. Returns the new flag."""
1097        launch = self.state.launch
1098        pos = self.state.position
1099        if launch is None or pos is None or pos.is_null():
1100            self.state.rtl_recommended = False
1101            return False
1102        range_m = self.battery_snapshot().get("range_m", 0.0)
1103        if range_m <= 0.0:
1104            # No usable range estimate yet (boat not making way) -> don't prompt.
1105            self.state.rtl_recommended = False
1106            return False
1107        from .core.geo import haversine_m
1108
1109        dist_home = haversine_m(pos, launch)
1110        recommend = range_m <= dist_home + self.config.safety.rtl_margin_m
1111        self.state.rtl_recommended = recommend
1112        if recommend and self.config.safety.auto_rtl and self.state.mode != ControlModeName.WAYPOINT:
1113            logger.warning("auto_rtl: battery range near distance-home; engaging RTL")
1114            self.return_to_launch()
1115        return recommend
1116
1117    def _load_route(self, command: dict) -> None:
1118        from .nav.routes import parse_gpx
1119
1120        try:
1121            if "gpx" in command:
1122                text = str(command["gpx"])
1123            elif "path" in command:
1124                with open(command["path"], "r", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
1125                    text = fh.read()
1126            else:
1127                logger.warning("load_route requires 'gpx' or 'path'")
1128                return
1129            waypoints = parse_gpx(text)
1130        except (ValueError, OSError) as exc:
1131            logger.warning("load_route failed: %s", exc)
1132            return
1133        self.state.waypoints = waypoints
1134        self.controller.handle_command(
1135            {"type": "load_route", "throttle": command.get("throttle")}
1136            if command.get("throttle") is not None
1137            else {"type": "load_route"}
1138        )
1139        logger.info("loaded route with %d waypoints", len(waypoints))
1140
1141    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1142    # Smart "Take me here" water routing (task #43)
1143    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1144    def plan_route(
1145        self, dest_lat: float, dest_lon: float, mode: str = "fastest", offset_m: float = 25.0
1146    ) -> dict:
1147        """Plan a water-only route from the boat's current position.
1148
1149        Synchronous and CPU/IO-heavy (Overpass fetch + shapely/networkx); the UI
1150        endpoint calls it in an executor. Returns the API contract dict. Does NOT
1151        start navigation.
1152        """
1153        from .nav import routing, water
1154
1155        # Fresh plan: clear any stale cancel request so a normal plan runs.
1156        self._route_plan_cancelled = False
1157        pos = self.state.position
1158        if pos is not None and not pos.is_null():
1159            start_lat, start_lon = pos.lat, pos.lon
1160        else:
1161            start_lat, start_lon = self.config.sim.start_lat, self.config.sim.start_lon
1162
1163        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1164        bbox = water.bbox_around(start_lat, start_lon, dest_lat, dest_lon)
1165        water_ll = cache.find_covering(bbox)
1166        if water_ll is None:
1167            try:
1168                elements = water.fetch_overpass(*bbox)
1169            except Exception as exc:  # network / endpoint failure
1170                logger.warning("water fetch failed: %s", exc)
1171                return {
1172                    "ok": False,
1173                    "waypoints": [],
1174                    "message": "No offline chart for this area; connect once to download it.",
1175                }
1176            water_ll = water.assemble_water(elements)
1177            if water_ll.is_empty:
1178                return {
1179                    "ok": False,
1180                    "waypoints": [],
1181                    "message": "No mapped water found around the route.",
1182                }
1183            try:
1184                cache.store(bbox, water_ll)
1185            except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1186                logger.warning("water cache store failed: %s", exc)
1187
1188        result = routing.plan_route(
1189            start_lat=start_lat,
1190            start_lon=start_lon,
1191            dest_lat=dest_lat,
1192            dest_lon=dest_lon,
1193            water_ll=water_ll,
1194            mode=mode,
1195            shoreline_offset_m=offset_m,
1196            cancelled=lambda: self._route_plan_cancelled,
1197        )
1198        return {
1199            "ok": result.ok,
1200            "waypoints": result.waypoints,
1201            "message": result.message,
1202        }
1203
1204    def cancel_route_plan(self) -> None:
1205        """Request that an in-progress route plan abort ASAP (#54)."""
1206        self._route_plan_cancelled = True
1207
1208    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1209    # "Around island" loop route (#77)
1210    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1211    def plan_island_loop(
1212        self, click_lat: float, click_lon: float, offset_m: float = 20.0
1213    ) -> dict:
1214        """Plan a closed loop route encircling the island under ``(lat, lon)``.
1215
1216        Uses the same offline water chart/cache as :meth:`plan_route` (fetches
1217        once if not cached). The boat's current position (or the sim start)
1218        decides which water body is the basin. Does NOT start navigation -- it
1219        returns waypoints for the route editor. Synchronous + CPU/IO-heavy; the
1220        UI endpoint calls it in an executor. Returns
1221        ``{ok, waypoints, loop, message}``.
1222        """
1223        from .nav import routing, water
1224
1225        pos = self.state.position
1226        if pos is not None and not pos.is_null():
1227            boat_lat, boat_lon = pos.lat, pos.lon
1228        else:
1229            boat_lat, boat_lon = self.config.sim.start_lat, self.config.sim.start_lon
1230
1231        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1232        bbox = water.bbox_around(boat_lat, boat_lon, click_lat, click_lon)
1233        water_ll = cache.find_covering(bbox)
1234        if water_ll is None:
1235            try:
1236                elements = water.fetch_overpass(*bbox)
1237            except Exception as exc:  # network / endpoint failure
1238                logger.warning("water fetch failed: %s", exc)
1239                return {
1240                    "ok": False,
1241                    "waypoints": [],
1242                    "loop": True,
1243                    "message": "No offline chart for this area; connect once to download it.",
1244                }
1245            water_ll = water.assemble_water(elements)
1246            if water_ll.is_empty:
1247                return {
1248                    "ok": False,
1249                    "waypoints": [],
1250                    "loop": True,
1251                    "message": "No mapped water found around the island.",
1252                }
1253            try:
1254                cache.store(bbox, water_ll)
1255            except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1256                logger.warning("water cache store failed: %s", exc)
1257
1258        result = routing.plan_island_loop(
1259            click_lat,
1260            click_lon,
1261            water_ll,
1262            boat_lat=boat_lat,
1263            boat_lon=boat_lon,
1264            offset_m=offset_m,
1265        )
1266        return {
1267            "ok": result.ok,
1268            "waypoints": result.waypoints,
1269            "loop": result.loop,
1270            "message": result.message,
1271        }
1272
1273    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1274    # Area survey "map mode" route (#47)
1275    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1276    def plan_survey(
1277        self, polygon_latlon: list, spacing_m: float, angle_deg: float | None = None
1278    ) -> dict:
1279        """Plan a boustrophedon coverage route over a closed area polygon.
1280
1281        Pure CPU work (shapely); the UI endpoint calls it in an executor. Does
1282        NOT start navigation -- it returns waypoints for the route editor.
1283        """
1284        from .nav import survey
1285
1286        try:
1287            result = survey.plan_survey(polygon_latlon, float(spacing_m), angle_deg)
1288        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
1289            logger.warning("survey plan failed: %s", exc)
1290            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": f"Bad survey request: {exc}"}
1291        return {
1292            "ok": result.ok,
1293            "waypoints": result.waypoints,
1294            "message": result.message,
1295        }
1296
1297    def plan_work_spots(self, polygon_latlon: list, spacing_m: float) -> dict:
1298        """Generate Work Area spots: an even serpentine grid over a drawn area,
1299        clipped to water (spots on land are dropped). Pure CPU (shapely) + the
1300        offline water cache; the UI endpoint calls it in an executor. Returns
1301        ``{ok, waypoints, message}`` -- the UI loads these as the Work Area spots."""
1302        from .nav import survey, water as water_mod
1303        from shapely.geometry import Point as _Pt
1304
1305        try:
1306            result = survey.plan_work_spots(polygon_latlon, float(spacing_m))
1307        except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
1308            logger.warning("work-area spots failed: %s", exc)
1309            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": f"Bad work-area request: {exc}"}
1310        if not result.ok:
1311            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [], "message": result.message}
1312
1313        # Clip spots to water (drop any over land) using the cached water polygon
1314        # for the area's bbox; if no water is cached, return the grid unclipped.
1315        wps = result.waypoints
1316        try:
1317            lats = [w["lat"] for w in wps]
1318            lons = [w["lon"] for w in wps]
1319            cache = water_mod.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1320            bbox = water_mod.bbox_around(min(lats), min(lons), max(lats), max(lons))
1321            geom = cache.find_covering(bbox)
1322            if geom is not None and not geom.is_empty:
1323                proj = water_mod.Projection.for_point(
1324                    (min(lons) + max(lons)) / 2, (min(lats) + max(lats)) / 2
1325                )
1326                water_m = proj.to_metric(geom)
1327                if not water_m.is_valid:
1328                    water_m = water_m.buffer(0)
1329                kept = [
1330                    w for w in wps
1331                    if water_m.covers(_Pt(*proj.point_to_metric(w["lon"], w["lat"])))
1332                ]
1333                if kept:  # only apply the clip if something survives (else keep all)
1334                    wps = [dict(w, name=f"Spot {i + 1}") for i, w in enumerate(kept)]
1335        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - clipping is best-effort
1336            logger.warning("work-area water clip skipped: %s", exc)
1337        return {"ok": True, "waypoints": wps,
1338                "message": f"{len(wps)} work spots." if wps else result.message}
1339
1340    def contour_route(self, lat: float, lon: float, window_m: float = 700.0) -> dict:
1341        """Build a route that follows the imported depth contour nearest
1342        (lat, lon), chaining same-depth pieces into a continuous track (a closed
1343        isobath comes back as a loop). Pure CPU (shapely); the UI endpoint calls it
1344        in an executor. Returns ``{ok, waypoints, depth_m, loop, message}`` -- the
1345        UI loads the waypoints as a route (patrol optional)."""
1346        from .nav import contour_route as cr
1347
1348        dlat = window_m / 111_320.0
1349        dlon = window_m / (111_320.0 * max(0.1, math.cos(math.radians(lat))))
1350        bbox = (lon - dlon, lat - dlat, lon + dlon, lat + dlat)  # (w, s, e, n)
1351        contours = self.depth_map.contours_in(bbox=bbox)
1352        if not contours:
1353            return {"ok": False, "waypoints": [],
1354                    "message": "No depth contours loaded around there."}
1355        return cr.contour_route_near(lat, lon, contours)
1356
1357    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1358    # Offline chart prefetch + management (#52)
1359    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
1360    def prefetch_chart(self, bbox: list) -> dict:
1361        """Fetch + cache the water polygon for a bbox so the boat can route
1362        offline later. ``bbox`` is ``[south, west, north, east]``.
1363
1364        Synchronous and IO-heavy (Overpass fetch); call it in an executor.
1365        Handles network failure gracefully.
1366        """
1367        from .nav import water
1368
1369        try:
1370            south, west, north, east = (float(v) for v in bbox)
1371        except (TypeError, ValueError):
1372            return {
1373                "ok": False,
1374                "cached": False,
1375                "vertices": 0,
1376                "message": "bbox must be [south, west, north, east].",
1377            }
1378        box = (south, west, north, east)
1379        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1380        existing = cache.find_covering(box)
1381        if existing is not None:
1382            return {
1383                "ok": True,
1384                "cached": True,
1385                "vertices": water._count_vertices(existing),
1386                "message": "Area already cached.",
1387            }
1388        try:
1389            elements = water.fetch_overpass(*box)
1390        except Exception as exc:  # network / endpoint failure
1391            logger.warning("chart prefetch fetch failed: %s", exc)
1392            return {
1393                "ok": False,
1394                "cached": False,
1395                "vertices": 0,
1396                "message": f"Could not download chart (offline?): {exc}",
1397            }
1398        water_ll = water.assemble_water(elements)
1399        if water_ll.is_empty:
1400            return {
1401                "ok": False,
1402                "cached": False,
1403                "vertices": 0,
1404                "message": "No mapped water found in that area.",
1405            }
1406        try:
1407            cache.store(box, water_ll)
1408        except OSError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - disk failure
1409            logger.warning("chart cache store failed: %s", exc)
1410            return {
1411                "ok": False,
1412                "cached": False,
1413                "vertices": water._count_vertices(water_ll),
1414                "message": f"Fetched chart but failed to cache it: {exc}",
1415            }
1416        return {
1417            "ok": True,
1418            "cached": True,
1419            "vertices": water._count_vertices(water_ll),
1420            "message": "Chart downloaded and cached for offline routing.",
1421        }
1422
1423    def list_charts(self) -> dict:
1424        """List cached chart bboxes + on-disk sizes (for the UI to show/manage)."""
1425        import json as _json
1426
1427        from .nav import water
1428
1429        cache = water.WaterCache(self.config.data_dir)
1430        charts: list[dict] = []
1431        if cache.dir.exists():
